Let's quickly recap just some the rights lost under this right wing Supreme Court of the United States ("SCOTUS"). At a minimum, Americans have LOST protections to:
•clean water
•voting rights
•reproductive health
•wrongful incarceration
•religion/state separation
•safety from gun violence
•4th amendment protections
•5th amendment protections
•anti-discrimination for being Queer
•racial justice in education and the work place
•economic justice for students suffering under predatory loans
And that's just a snapshot, and now as the Supreme Court tacitly endorses Trump's move to disappear Venezuelan immigrants to El Salvador prisons, you get the idea on how dire the situation really is. Meanwhile, I keep hearing comments alleging that _this_ Supreme Court has dishonored a once honorable Court. The truth is actually far worse than what most realize. The truth is that the right wing extremism we're seeing out of the Supreme Court is not an aberration, but a manifestation, of Supreme Court extremism throughout U.S. history. The receipts are devastating as they are ignored by corporate media. **Let 's Address This.**
**Let's Address This is made possibly by you --the thoughtful person reading this. Add your email below and subscribe to our movement for justice.**
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..NxtMWygquPOHO-vqPKgLvV71ANYssacBr5bJVicpj_4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=160930478)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/622ac9fa-2dc7-4230-a53c-d854864b4f8a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Current Supreme Court Justices. [Credit Supreme Court Website]
## A History of Injustice
In reality, SCOTUS has (almost) _never_ reflected the will of the majority of American people. In fact, it has most often blocked what Americans want and voted for. And while no one article can do justice to a history that began on March 4, 1789 when the Supreme Court was first established, this snapshot provides a clear picture of what we're up against, and why this is unsustainable.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..NxtMWygquPOHO-vqPKgLvV71ANYssacBr5bJVicpj_4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=160930478)
## 19th Century Injustice
**Let 's start in 1857 with Dred Scott v. Sanford.** SCOTUS ruled 7-2 that Black people are subhuman and therefore cannot be citizens. This ruling prevented a peaceful or legal solution to ending slavery, and historians cite this case as one of the final straws that likely enabled the eventual Civil War. At least 600,000 soldiers died in the Civil War. The subsequent Jim Crow laws and the harm they cause reverberate to this day.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/27f01783-12c4-4d71-a915-723bbca5f5cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Dred and Harriett Scott [Credit: NPS.Gov]
**In 1883 the Court advanced this racist policy with The Civil Rights Cases.** Most Americans don't realize that some 90 years before the 1964 Civil Rights Act, Congress passed the Civil Rights Act of 1875 to mandate equal justice for Black Americans. Instead, SCOTUS ruled 8-1 that the Civil Rights Act of 1875 was unconstitutional, and that the 13th and 14th Amendments did not actually provide equal protection to Black people. Hence Jim Crow laws were not only valid, but now had full mandate to strengthen and advance as Black Codes. This barbarity would stay on the books until 1964, devastating generations of Black Americans.
**In 1896 the Court further enforced Black Codes under Plessy v. Ferguson.** SCOTUS ruled 7-1 that mandatory racial segregation was not in violation of the 14th Amendment. This further advanced Black codes, Jim Crow, economic, housing, and educational disenfranchisement. This SCOTUS ruling further strengthened the KKK and white supremacy. The KKK grew so powerful as a result of rulings like Plessy, that by 1920 they were 4 million member strong, and at least 1 in every 11 white Americans was a card carrying member.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/894124aa-eea8-4d62-bf89-35e1c12a1e87?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Credit: Harvey Fireside
> **SideBar**
> _But Qasim!_ Some might argue. _That was a long time ago in the 19th century! Surely you can 't be serious that the 20th and 21st century was that bad? _
> In response I'd say, _I am serious, and don 't call me Shirley. And keep reading to see._
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..NxtMWygquPOHO-vqPKgLvV71ANYssacBr5bJVicpj_4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=160930478)
## 20th Century Injustice
**In 1918 the Court embraced child abuse via child labor in Hammer v. Dagenhart.** SCOTUS ruled 5-4 that Congress could not ban child labor in intrastate commerce, calling it a "states rights" issue. Sound familiar? As we see labor cases come before SCOTUS today, and see GOP led states pass laws enabling child labor, remember this history of enabling child labor and child abuse. For example, right now in 2025 Florida is pushing to loosen child labor laws to allow 14-year-old children to enter the work force and even work overnight shifts. Again, what we are seeing today is not new, but a revival of yesteryear extremism.
**In 1924 the court targeted Asian Americans directly in Thind v. United States.** SCOTUS ruled unanimously, 9-0, that though a Sikh American named Bhagat Thind was Aryan per the U.S. then phrenological and eugenicist pseudoscience definition of white, he did not meet a "common sense" definition of white--and therefore could _not_ be a citizen. This ruling helped uphold a 1924 total ban on all Asian immigration. That ban would remain on the books for another 41 years until the 1965 Immigration and Nationality Act ended these racist race based quotas. It's worth noting that for much of American history, while Black, Latino, and Asian immigration was heavily restricted or outright banned, white European immigration was largely encouraged via open borders.
**Side Note:** When you hear white MAGAs scream "_immigrants should come here legally_ ," know that their own ancestors arrived here illegally per their own made up definition of legality and illegality. But that's an article for another time.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/628e7fc9-003f-4f58-8f21-23297bc6150b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Bhagat Singh Thind [Credit: The Pluralism Project at Harvard University]
**The 1927 Supreme Court continued its eugenics streak in Buck v. Bell.** SCOTUS ruled 8-1 to uphold a Virginia eugenics law targeting Black people. States nationwide would then adopt this ruling and craft their own state laws. As a result, more than 60,000 Black women, men, and children as young as 10 were forcibly sterilized--which we now recognize as a textbook definition of genocide. Perhaps just as horrifying is that as of 2025 Buck v. Bell remains valid Supreme Court precedent as no subsequent SCOTUS ruling has ever overturned it. Given how the Trump regime is using the 1798 Alien Enemies Act to disappear brown migrants, I do not put it past him to use the 1927 Buck v. Bell ruling to revive genocide of Black Americans.
**In 1944, while American soldiers fought fascism abroad, the Supreme Court ruled in favor of fascism in Korematsu v. United States.** SCOTUS ruled 6-3 to uphold President FDR's Executive Order 9066 that forcibly interned 120,000 Japanese Americans. Though America was at war with Japan, Germany, _and_ Italy--only Japanese Americans suffered mass arrest and deprivation of property. In broad daylight, our government spoke of the evils of Hitler's concentration camps, while placing American citizens in concentration camps of our own--reminding everyone once more that Hitler got his ideas for concentration camps from the British Boer camps and from American Jim Crow. (The must read book on this topic is [Hitler's American Model by Professor James Q. Whitman](https://substack.com/redirect/5f9de290-0013-44ab-a75d-25f13b8e16b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)).
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/607cb435-ced5-4f14-a252-e8929f70fc11?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Fred T. Koremotsu [Credit: SFUSD.edu]
**In 1986 the Supreme Court ruled in Bowers v. Hardwick to condemn queer Americans.** SCOTUS ruled 5-4 to uphold a Georgia statute criminalizing even private LGBTQ relationships. Though finally overruled in Lawrence v Texas in 2005, anti-sodomy laws still exist in several states, anti-LGBTQ violence and rhetoric remains at record highs, [and it is getting worse](https://substack.com/redirect/657954fa-17b2-4d5a-b5b1-801fba1cee7c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> **Sidebar**
> Far from improving significantly over the 19th century, in the 20th century the Supreme Court openly supported white supremacy, abusive child labor practices, anti-Asian immigration bans, eugenics, genocide, fascism, and anti-LGBTQ bigotry. And while we celebrate the Civil Rights acts passed in the 20th century, we've seen a massive regression of those very laws in the 21st century. So, if you thought the 21st century Court was better than past eras, read on, because sadly, it is not.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..NxtMWygquPOHO-vqPKgLvV71ANYssacBr5bJVicpj_4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=160930478)
## 21st Century Injustice
**In 2000 the Supreme Court ruled on Bush v. Gore, denying American people their vote.** SCOTUS ruled 7-2 in favor of GW Bush, handing him the presidency. This was despite Al Gore winning the popular vote by more than 500K votes. Bush then bankrupted our surplus, cut billionaire taxes, left America trillions in debt, and waged two failed wars that cost more than $6 trillion and killed tens of thousands of American soldiers and hundreds of thousands of Afghan and Iraqi civilians. I bring up Bush's atrocious record to document the consequences of the Supreme Court installing someone the American people voted against.
**In 2008 the Supreme Court voted to devastated our climate and prop up corporate corruption in Exxon Shipping Co. v. Baker.** SCOTUS ruled 5-3 to protect Exxon from punitive damages in which a drunk operator spilled 11 million gallons of oil into an ecological sensitive sound. SCOTUS dropped the fine from $5 billion to only $500 million, boosting Exxon's value by $23 billion. It is worth nothing that [Justice Alito owned Exxon stock](https://substack.com/redirect/a4d47b54-9c41-4eff-82ac-270d95e7808e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and profited massively from the Court's ruling.
**In 2010 the Supreme Court helped ensure we 'd never have a free and fair election again by ruling on Citizens United v. FEC.** SCOTUS ruled 5-4 that the First Amendment protects political donations as "free speech," opening elections to billions in dark money to buy and sell politicians. Though [an astounding 83% of Americans--Republicans, Independents, and Democrats alike--want corporate money out of politics](https://substack.com/redirect/dd3b0380-c88f-45dc-9c5f-bd2f15ed662c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), SCOTUS does not care. As a result of this overt power to buy elections, [billionaires spent $1.2B in the 2020 election](https://substack.com/redirect/7735e023-ea05-4778-82f8-7cef7393becd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which is an astounding 70 times more than what they spent in the 2008 election--pre-Citizens United. In the 2024 election, maniacal billionaire Elon Musk spent $175M of his own money to elect Trump, and is now wielding that influence to gut life critical departments including HHS, Social Security, Medicaid, Medicare, and more. The Supreme Court's ruling ensured corrupt corporate funded politicians stay in power with ease, State and Congressional level gerrymandering becomes the norm, and gave the wealthy a nearly insurmountable advantage to stay in office.
**In 2013 the Supreme Court further eroded our elections in Shelby County v. Holder by stripping down voting rights.** SCOTUS ruled 5-4 to gut the Voting Rights Act of 1964--specifically provisions that prevented historically discriminatory states and counties from disenfranchising BIPOC voters. Unsurprinsgly, after Shelby, [red states closed thousands of polling locations](https://substack.com/redirect/1849167b-72d1-4fa8-8b07-7bcce431919f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in disproportionately BIPOC neighborhoods, affecting turnout and flipping elections red. [GOP states have subsequently introduced hundreds of voter suppression bills](https://substack.com/redirect/66116444-75c9-488a-9839-9cb76ab44fa4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), passing dozens, further marginalizing Black, brown, and low income voters. In fact, since the Shelby ruling, red states have closed more than 100,000 polling locations in disproportionately low income, Black, and Latino neighborhoods. Modern day Jim Crow by any other name.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..NxtMWygquPOHO-vqPKgLvV71ANYssacBr5bJVicpj_4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=160930478)
**In 2016 the Supreme Court advanced government tyranny in Utah v. Strieff.** SCOTUS ruled 5-3 to permit police to admit evidence in a criminal trial even if it was procured by illegal searches--despite 4th Amendment ban on illegal government searches. This ruling particularly targets BIPOC communities who already face [at least six times higher charge/arrest rates](https://substack.com/redirect/9a59e2f2-aedc-40b9-a77d-cfbfd2e337e4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) than do white Americans. Now, police officers can more easily procure evidence through illegal means, blurring the line between the right to privacy, and making it more difficult to stop bad police actors from planting evidence or even targeting citizens in violation of the Fourth Amendment. This ruling was a boost for fascism and for the private prison industry.
**In 2018 the Supreme Court advanced religious bigotry in Trump v. Hawaii.** SCOTUS ruled 5-4 to uphold Trump's racist & discriminatory "Muslim Ban." A ban he admitted was designed to cause as much harm as possible, and one he could not prove had any national security value. So much for US Constitution's ban on religious tests.
**In 2021 the Supreme Court shockingly defended child slavery in Nestl e USA, Inc. v. Doe I.** SCOTUS ruled near unanimously, 8-1, that children kidnapped, enslaved and forced to go to the Ivory Coast to produce for American corporate giants like Nestle, could not sue Nestle for damages for enabling child slave labor. Yes, it is as horrific as it sounds. Nearly 175 years after ruling in Dred Scott that enslaved Black people are subhuman and therefore unworthy of protection from exploitation by American corporations, the Court found a way to rule in Nestle that enslaved African children are unworthy of protection from exploitation by American corporations.
**In 2022 the Supreme Court undid half a century of precedent in Dobbs v. Jackson Women 's Health Organization.** SCOTUS ruled 6-3 that women do not have autonomy over their own bodies. The Court repealed Roe--despite 72% of Americans favoring Roe, despite all the data that abortion bans do not decrease abortion rates but increase maternal mortality by 24%. The Court repealed Roe, despite four justices--Alito, Gorsuch, Kavanaugh, and Barrett--affirming that they viewed Roe as settled law, settled precedent, and the law of the land.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4fe3f39f-2243-4878-b216-b2b2023c0216?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..NxtMWygquPOHO-vqPKgLvV71ANYssacBr5bJVicpj_4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=160930478)
**In 2023, the Supreme Court repealed Affirmative Action.** The SCOTUS ignored the clearly racist Legacy Admissions policy which disproportionately benefits wealthy white people, but gutted Affirmative Action because it marginally helps Black and brown people (even though the single largest demographic of people who benefit from Affirmative Action are white women--more than Black, Latino, Indigenous, and Asian people _combined_).
**In 2024, the Supreme Court granted Presidents virtually unlimited immunity.** SCOTUS ruled on the question of whether Trump has immunity as President from accountability for otherwise criminal acts, and essentially granted him near unlimited immunity. So much for no Kings?
**And now in 2025, the Supreme Court tacitly approves the destruction of Due Process as Trump disappears immigrants.** Justice Sotomayor wrote in her scathing dissent of this fascist ruling:
> The Government takes the position that, even when it makes a mistake, it cannot retrieve individuals from the Salvadoran prisons to which it has sent them. The implication of the Government's position is that not only noncitizens **but also United States citizens could be taken off the streets** , forced onto planes, and confined to foreign prisons with no opportunity for redress if judicial review is denied unlawfully before removal. History is no stranger to such lawless regimes, but this Nation's system of laws is designed to prevent, not enable, their rise.
## The Future of SCOTUS Injustice
Indeed, the 21st century SCOTUS has largely continued the SCOTUS trend of the 19th and 20th centuries of ruling against the will of the American people, and in favor of the wealthy and powerful. In addition to overturning presidential elections, stripping down voting rights, destroying our climate, devastating the right to privacy, and destroying personal bodily autonomy, SCOTUS in the 21st century has also further enabled wrongful incarceration, further dissolved religion/state separation, further decreased safety from gun violence, further enabled discrimination for being Queer, stripped down affirmative action and racial justice in education and the work place, and further harmed economic justice for students suffering under predatory loans.
**Again, I could go on.**
But I believe I've made the point. And history reminds us what's at stake. I've long argued that we must enforce term limits and expand the Court--and indeed Biden could have made that push but chose not to.
## Conclusion
When the Court overruled the 1875 Civil Rights cases, it took nearly a century--until 1964--for those rights to finally be codified once more. Now, only 60 years later those rights are once again being stripped down piece by piece--by the Supreme Court no less. Who knows how long it will take us to recover? Indeed this is why the late great John Lewis said:
> Ours is not the struggle of one day, one week, or one year. Ours is not the struggle of one judicial appointment or presidential term. Ours is the struggle of a lifetime, or maybe even many lifetimes, and each one of us in every generation must do our part.
Whether by organizing, donating, volunteering, voting, or combatting disinformation, or all of the above, let us do our part to protect and preserve our democracy for this generation, and the next. Because far from protecting our rights, we can tragically only count on the current Supreme Court to be among the greatest obstacles to justice in America.
**I am doing everything in my power to raise awareness on human rights and justice. I cannot do it alone. I hope you consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.**

As a result, more than 60,000 Black women, men, and children as young as 10 were forcibly sterilized--which we now recognize as a textbook definition of genocide. Perhaps just as horrifying is that as of 2025 Buck v. Bell remains valid Supreme Court precedent as no subsequent SCOTUS ruling has ever overturned it. Given how the Trump regime is using the 1798 Alien Enemies Act to disappear brown migrants, I do not put it past him to use the 1927 Buck v. Bell ruling to revive genocide of Black Americans. In 1944, while American soldiers fought fascism abroad, the Supreme Court ruled in favor of fascism in Korematsu v. United States. SCOTUS ruled 6-3 to uphold President FDR's Executive Order 9066 that forcibly interned 120,000 Japanese Americans. Though America was at war with Japan, Germany, and Italy--only Japanese Americans suffered mass arrest and deprivation of property. In broad daylight, our government spoke of the evils of Hitler's concentration camps, while placing American citizens in concentration camps of our own--reminding everyone once more that Hitler got his ideas for concentration camps from the British Boer camps and from American Jim Crow. (The must read book on this topic is ). | | ---|---|--- Fred T. Koremotsu [Credit: SFUSD.edu] In 1986 the Supreme Court ruled in Bowers v. Hardwick to condemn queer Americans. SCOTUS ruled 5-4 to uphold a Georgia statute criminalizing even private LGBTQ relationships. Though finally overruled in Lawrence v Texas in 2005, anti-sodomy laws still exist in several states, anti-LGBTQ violence and rhetoric remains at record highs, . Sidebar Far from improving significantly over the 19th century, in the 20th century the Supreme Court openly supported white supremacy, abusive child labor practices, anti-Asian immigration bans, eugenics, genocide, fascism, and anti-LGBTQ bigotry. And while we celebrate the Civil Rights acts passed in the 20th century, we've seen a massive regression of those very laws in the 21st century. So, if you thought the 21st century Court was better than past eras, read on, because sadly, it is not. 21st Century Injustice In 2000 the Supreme Court ruled on Bush v. Gore, denying American people their vote. SCOTUS ruled 7-2 in favor of GW Bush, handing him the presidency. This was despite Al Gore winning the popular vote by more than 500K votes. Bush then bankrupted our surplus, cut billionaire taxes, left America trillions in debt, and waged two failed wars that cost more than $6 trillion and killed tens of thousands of American soldiers and hundreds of thousands of Afghan and Iraqi civilians. I bring up Bush's atrocious record to document the consequences of the Supreme Court installing someone the American people voted against. In 2008 the Supreme Court voted to devastated our climate and prop up corporate corruption in Exxon Shipping Co. v. Baker. SCOTUS ruled 5-3 to protect Exxon from punitive damages in which a drunk operator spilled 11 million gallons of oil into an ecological sensitive sound. SCOTUS dropped the fine from $5 billion to only $500 million, boosting Exxon's value by $23 billion. It is worth nothing that and profited massively from the Court's ruling. In 2010 the Supreme Court helped ensure we 'd never have a free and fair election again by ruling on Citizens United v. FEC. SCOTUS ruled 5-4 that the First Amendment protects political donations as "free speech," opening elections to billions in dark money to buy and sell politicians. Though , SCOTUS does not care. As a result of this overt power to buy elections, , which is an astounding 70 times more than what they spent in the 2008 election--pre-Citizens United. In the 2024 election, maniacal billionaire Elon Musk spent $175M of his own money to elect Trump, and is now wielding that influence to gut life critical departments including HHS, Social Security, Medicaid, Medicare, and more. The Supreme Court's ruling ensured corrupt corporate funded politicians stay in power with ease, State and Congressional level gerrymandering becomes the norm, and gave the wealthy a nearly insurmountable advantage to stay in office. In 2013 the Supreme Court further eroded our elections in Shelby County v. Holder by stripping down voting rights. SCOTUS ruled 5-4 to gut the Voting Rights Act of 1964--specifically provisions that prevented historically discriminatory states and counties from disenfranchising BIPOC voters. Unsurprinsgly, after Shelby, in disproportionately BIPOC neighborhoods, affecting turnout and flipping elections red. , passing dozens, further marginalizing Black, brown, and low income voters. In fact, since the Shelby ruling, red states have closed more than 100,000 polling locations in disproportionately low income, Black, and Latino neighborhoods. Modern day Jim Crow by any other name. In 2016 the Supreme Court advanced government tyranny in Utah v. Strieff. SCOTUS ruled 5-3 to permit police to admit evidence in a criminal trial even if it was procured by illegal searches--despite 4th Amendment ban on illegal government searches. This ruling particularly targets BIPOC communities who already face than do white Americans. Now, police officers can more easily procure evidence through illegal means, blurring the line between the right to privacy, and making it more difficult to stop bad police actors from planting evidence or even targeting citizens in violation of the Fourth Amendment. This ruling was a boost for fascism and for the private prison industry. In 2018 the Supreme Court advanced religious bigotry in Trump v. Hawaii. SCOTUS ruled 5-4 to uphold Trump's racist & discriminatory "Muslim Ban." A ban he admitted was designed to cause as much harm as possible, and one he could not prove had any national security value. So much for US Constitution's ban on religious tests. In 2021 the Supreme Court shockingly defended child slavery in Nestl e USA, Inc. v. Doe I. SCOTUS ruled near unanimously, 8-1, that children kidnapped, enslaved and forced to go to the Ivory Coast to produce for American corporate giants like Nestle, could not sue Nestle for damages for enabling child slave labor. Yes, it is as horrific as it sounds. Nearly 175 years after ruling in Dred Scott that enslaved Black people are subhuman and therefore unworthy of protection from exploitation by American corporations, the Court found a way to rule in Nestle that enslaved African children are unworthy of protection from exploitation by American corporations. In 2022 the Supreme Court undid half a century of precedent in Dobbs v. Jackson Women 's Health Organization. SCOTUS ruled 6-3 that women do not have autonomy over their own bodies. The Court repealed Roe--despite 72% of Americans favoring Roe, despite all the data that abortion bans do not decrease abortion rates but increase maternal mortality by 24%. The Court repealed Roe, despite four justices--Alito, Gorsuch, Kavanaugh, and Barrett--affirming that they viewed Roe as settled law, settled precedent, and the law of the land. | | ---|---|--- In 2023, the Supreme Court repealed Affirmative Action. The SCOTUS ignored the clearly racist Legacy Admissions policy which disproportionately benefits wealthy white people, but gutted Affirmative Action because it marginally helps Black and brown people (even though the single largest demographic of people who benefit from Affirmative Action are white women--more than Black, Latino, Indigenous, and Asian people combined). In 2024, the Supreme Court granted Presidents virtually unlimited immunity. SCOTUS ruled on the question of whether Trump has immunity as President from accountability for otherwise criminal acts, and essentially granted him near unlimited immunity. So much for no Kings? And now in 2025, the Supreme Court tacitly approves the destruction of Due Process as Trump disappears immigrants. Justice Sotomayor wrote in her scathing dissent of this fascist ruling: The Government takes the position that, even when it makes a mistake, it cannot retrieve individuals from the Salvadoran prisons to which it has sent them. The implication of the Government's position is that not only noncitizens but also United States citizens could be taken off the streets , forced onto planes, and confined to foreign prisons with no opportunity for redress if judicial review is denied unlawfully before removal. History is no stranger to such lawless regimes, but this Nation's system of laws is designed to prevent, not enable, their rise. The Future of SCOTUS Injustice Indeed, the 21st century SCOTUS has largely continued the SCOTUS trend of the 19th and 20th centuries of ruling against the will of the American people, and in favor of the wealthy and powerful. In addition to overturning presidential elections, stripping down voting rights, destroying our climate, devastating the right to privacy, and destroying personal bodily autonomy, SCOTUS in the 21st century has also further enabled wrongful incarceration, further dissolved religion/state separation, further decreased safety from gun violence, further enabled discrimination for being Queer, stripped down affirmative action and racial justice in education and the work place, and further harmed economic justice for students suffering under predatory loans. Again, I could go on. But I believe I've made the point. And history reminds us what's at stake. I've long argued that we must enforce term limits and expand the Court--and indeed Biden could have made that push but chose not to. Conclusion When the Court overruled the 1875 Civil Rights cases, it took nearly a century--until 1964--for those rights to finally be codified once more. Now, only 60 years later those rights are once again being stripped down piece by piece--by the Supreme Court no less. Who knows how long it will take us to recover? Indeed this is why the late great John Lewis said: Ours is not the struggle of one day, one week, or one year. Ours is not the struggle of one judicial appointment or presidential term. Ours is the struggle of a lifetime, or maybe even many lifetimes, and each one of us in every generation must do our part. Whether by organizing, donating, volunteering, voting, or combatting disinformation, or all of the above, let us do our part to protect and preserve our democracy for this generation, and the next. Because far from protecting our rights, we can tragically only count on the current Supreme Court to be among the greatest obstacles to justice in America. I am doing everything in my power to raise awareness on human rights and justice. I cannot do it alone. I hope you consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. | | ---|---|--- Quick Ask! I am doing everything in my power over the next four years to stave off fascism in the United States, and I need your support.Become a paid subscriber and join the movement.** | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Qasim Rashid, Esq. 1552 South Route 59 #1333 Naperville, IL 60564 ? ?

name: Voter Action Project
subject: Tell Congress to stop Trump's plan to privatize the USPS
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project
Folks -- We know Donald Trump is attempting to take over the United States Postal Service and make it part of the Commerce Department. But now we know his true endgame.
Trump and his greedy billionaire buddies on Wall Street are trying to fully privatize the USPS. Their goal is simple: break it apart, and sell it off for its parts to the highest corporate bidder that has zero interest in continuing to serve every ZIP code.
Now, we have a real opportunity to have Congress take a stand and vow to stop Trump’s USPS privatization plan and save the people’s post office .
Almost 200 members of Congress have already co-sponsored bipartisan resolutions that would draw a clear line in the sand that Congress intends to take every measure to ensure the Postal Service remains an independent public institution.
Tell your representatives in Congress to support the bipartisan bills in the House and Senate to stop this shady privatization scheme before it's too late >> [https://actionnetwork.org/letters/save-the-usps-3/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.everyaction.com%2F9bivrSvjFEim0QMMvgcQrA2]
SIGN HERE NOW ⇨ [https://actionnetwork.org/letters/save-the-usps-3/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.everyaction.com%2F9bivrSvjFEim0QMMvgcQrA2]
H.Res.70 and S.Res.147 reaffirm the founding principle that the USPS exists to serve the people, not greedy corporate billionaires . These resolutions are proof that protecting our public post office is a bipartisan concern, with support from Democrats and Republicans alike. From Republican Rep. Don Bacon and Sen. Thom Tillis, to Democratic Sen. Maggie Hassan and Rep. Gerry Connolly, lawmakers across deep red and blue districts are rejecting privatization.
Leaders in both political parties know exactly who privatization would hurt: rural families, veterans, seniors, postal workers, Black-owned small businesses, Indigenous communities, and many more. The loss of the people’s post office and the public services it provides to folks across the country would be devastating.
Trump and his billionaire cronies tried dismantling the post office for their own profit before -- and we stopped them . But now they’re back with an even more aggressive plan. We can’t stay silent, Congress must hear from us that we will not allow a hostile takeover of the people’s post office to succeed. The damage will be irreversible.
Congress must act before it’s too late. This is our chance to once again show Wall Street our broad, bipartisan support for the defense of the Postal Service from being gutted for corporate gain.
Urge your members of Congress to co-sponsor and pass bipartisan H.Res.70 and S.Res.147 to stop Trump's privatization plan now! >> [https://actionnetwork.org/letters/save-the-usps-3/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.everyaction.com%2F9bivrSvjFEim0QMMvgcQrA2]
SIGN HERE NOW ⇨ [https://actionnetwork.org/letters/save-the-usps-3/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.everyaction.com%2F9bivrSvjFEim0QMMvgcQrA2]

https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2]
Donate Now [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=20&amtOpts=&recurringAm=20&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&contactdata=98SWl8gSn/g/pyky+mLIYE9JTphW9dG3ImRZ4wvb4qGUjRrWDwXJzGaYNQNFjpMPoA7YEqZsJDiO582+3gL+i0vw8gg96UTBklA4d7/fKIM=]

name: Elizabeth Nader
subject: 62 days
body:
Peter, The New Jersey Primary Election is 62 days away. Two short months. THIS is our opportunity to decide who ends up on our November ballot. More of the same, status-quo keepers who live in the pocket of the elit
| | [ ]()
---
Peter, The New Jersey Primary Election is**62 days away.** _**Two short months.**_ THIS is our opportunity to decide who ends up on our November ballot. More of the same, status-quo keepers who live in the pocket of the elite establishment? Or bold, decisive candidates of conviction who will answer to us first and lead with common sense? The choice is ours. And to make it, we must participate. I have been urging you for months to make a plan to vote, to make sure you’re registered, to apply to vote by mail, and all that. I can hope at this point that you’ve done that, and now I need you to help us identify more voters like you and help them make that same step. To do that, we need funding. The establishment has it. To go toe-to-toe and get Common Sense New Jerseyans out to vote, we need dollars too.
---

Hello, and thank you for taking the time to read this email. This message is part of an ongoing conversation between us, and I want to take a moment to ensure you have full transparency about why you received this message, how your information is handled, and what to expect from future emails. Why You’re Receiving This Email You’re receiving this email because at some point, you opted in to receive updates, news, or information on a specific topic we’ve previously discussed or shared. Whether it was through a subscription, a form submission, or another form of communication, your information was shared willingly, and we respect your decision to connect with us. If you’re wondering about the nature of our correspondence, rest assured that we aim to keep all emails relevant, timely, and free from unnecessary clutter. This includes respecting your inbox and refraining from sending irrelevant messages. Your Privacy is Important We value your trust and take your privacy very seriously. The information you provide is securely stored and never shared, sold, or used outside the purpose for which you provided it. If you ever want to review how we handle your data, feel free to contact us directly. Transparency is our priority, and we’re happy to address any questions you might have. You are always in control of the information you share. If you feel that any part of your subscription or interaction needs clarification, let us know. We’re here to provide accurate answers and ensure your satisfaction. How to Unsubscribe or Manage Your Preferences We understand that everyone’s inbox is different. If you ever find our emails no longer relevant, there’s no hard feelings. You can easily manage your email preferences or unsubscribe using the link provided below. By clicking the unsubscribe link, you’ll be taken to a page where you can either adjust your communication preferences (such as receiving fewer emails or only on specific topics) or completely remove yourself from our list. The process is straightforward, and any changes will take effect promptly. We don’t use tricks or gimmicks to keep you subscribed. Our priority is to ensure that our emails add value to your day, and if they don’t, we respect your decision to part ways. Accessibility and Communication If you have any trouble accessing the unsubscribe page, managing preferences, or understanding why you’re receiving this email, you can reach out directly to our support team at info@commonsense.org. We aim to provide a response within a reasonable time frame and address your concerns effectively. Our communication is designed to be as inclusive as possible, but we know there’s always room for improvement. If you have any feedback on how we can make our emails more accessible or relevant, please don’t hesitate to share. A Commitment to Non-Intrusive Emails We aim to create a non-intrusive communication experience. This means we won’t overwhelm your inbox with excessive messages, and we work hard to ensure our content remains clear and concise. The purpose of this email is to stay connected with you and provide updates or information that we believe is meaningful. If we ever fail to meet these standards, we encourage you to let us know. Feedback, whether positive or constructive, is always appreciated. Your thoughts help us understand how we can do better and improve our communication approach. While we cannot guarantee every suggestion will be implemented, we will take the time to carefully review and consider your input. Contact Information If you’d like to get in touch with us outside of managing your preferences, here’s how you can reach us: Email: info@commonsense.org
Phone: (866) 916-3221 (please note this is a text-only line)
Mailing Address:
Common Sense Club
165 Route 35
Red Bank, New Jersey, 07701 We strive to ensure all communication channels are open and readily available to you. Whether it’s a question, comment, or concern, our team is ready to assist. Legal Information We comply with all applicable laws and regulations regarding email communication. This includes adhering to anti-spam laws and maintaining the highest standards for consent-based communication. Your trust matters, and we work diligently to ensure every email you receive meets these requirements. If you need further clarification on our compliance policies or legal obligations, please feel free to ask. Transparency and accountability are central to our communication strategy, and we’re happy to provide additional details if needed. A Final Note Emails are one of the many ways we stay connected, but we understand they aren’t perfect for everyone. If there’s another way you’d prefer to communicate or stay updated, let us know. Whether it’s through social media, a direct call, or another channel, we’re open to finding the most effective way to share information with you. We want to emphasize that our goal is never to disrupt or clutter your inbox. Every email sent is intended to provide value, and we genuinely appreciate your time and attention. Thank you for allowing us to stay in touch with you. This footer was designed to ensure transparency, provide essential information, and give you full control over your communication preferences. We hope it meets your expectations, but if there’s anything you’d like to see improved, we’re always here to listen.
---

A Final Note Emails are one of the many ways we stay connected, but we understand they aren’t perfect for everyone. If there’s another way you’d prefer to communicate or stay updated, let us know. Whether it’s through social media, a direct call, or another channel, we’re open to finding the most effective way to share information with you. We want to emphasize that our goal is never to disrupt or clutter your inbox. Every email sent is intended to provide value, and we genuinely appreciate your time and attention. Thank you for allowing us to stay in touch with you. This footer was designed to ensure transparency, provide essential information, and give you full control over your communication preferences. We hope it meets your expectations, but if there’s anything you’d like to see improved, we’re always here to listen. Common Sense Club 165 Route 35 Red Bank, New Jersey 07701 United States

name: Tom Barrett
subject: Big News
body:
Thank you for your help | | | [](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=67f7dd685e1ada23e2975ef1&u=https%3A%2F%2Ftombarrettforcongress.com%2F%3Fss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D67f7bb0f5e1ada23e28ff8c9%26ss_email_id%3D67f7dd685e1ada23e2975ef1%26ss_campaign_name%3DBig%2BNews%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2025-04-10T15%253A06%253A14Z&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_67f7bb0f5e1ada23e28ff8c9&l=en-US&s=O03vfYDO-syStY0wHqqqgdow4Lw%3D)
---
| **Huge Q1 Fundraising Report** _The first quarter fundraising deadline has passed and I am proud to announce that**we raised more than $800,000** \- a monster haul thanks to supporters like you.__Washington Democrats are working feverishly to recruit my opponent and this fundraising accomplishment will make it that much harder.__Thank you!_
---
[_ _**DONATE** _ _](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=67f7dd685e1ada23e2975ef1&u=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.winred.com%2Ftom-barrett-for-congress%2Fapril10&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_67f7bb0f5e1ada23e28ff8c9&l=en-US&s=1NyJCLfWN8ISwHD_HOVv-SS-7Q0%3D)
---
**Democrats are Coming for Me** _This week, the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee announced that I am one of their top targets for 2026..._
---
| [](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=67f7dd685e1ada23e2975ef1&u=https%3A%2F%2Fpunchbowl.news%2Fdccc-target-list%2F%3Futm_campaign%3D4%252F8%252F25%2520%2520AM%253A%26utm_medium%3Demail%26utm_source%3DSailthru%26utm_term%3DPunchbowl%2520AM%2520and%2520Active%2520Subscribers%2520from%2520Memberful%2520Combined&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_67f7bb0f5e1ada23e28ff8c9&l=en-US&s=Ka_aQqKG3zYAynnGMGKk0PzGXKY%3D)
---
_But that’s not all._
* _[Larry Sabato’s Crystal Ball](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=67f7dd685e1ada23e2975ef1&u=https%3A%2F%2Fcenterforpolitics.org%2Fcrystalball%2Fthe-house-democrats-favored-on-what-starts-as-a-small-battlefield%2F&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_67f7bb0f5e1ada23e28ff8c9&l=en-US&s=rPSc5aWBPYUbCRRqv0QAhx7VBYs%3D) has me listed as one of 13 Republican-held seats that are now “Toss Ups.”_
* _The[Cook Political Report](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=67f7dd685e1ada23e2975ef1&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.cookpolitical.com%2Fratings%2Fhouse-race-ratings&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_67f7bb0f5e1ada23e28ff8c9&l=en-US&s=PPwWSsY4_rTvMzFKEF7VpU96r48%3D) moved MI-7 from a +2 Republican seat to “Even.”_
* _The progressive[House Majority PAC](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=67f7dd685e1ada23e2975ef1&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.thehousemajoritypac.com%2Fnews%2Fmemo-hmp-2026-recruitment-fund&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_67f7bb0f5e1ada23e28ff8c9&l=en-US&s=kpgWjPt_DTPz8gte0XxMrOoJ8CQ%3D) has named me a top target._

---
[_ _**DONATE** _ _](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=67f7dd685e1ada23e2975ef1&u=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.winred.com%2Ftom-barrett-for-congress%2Fapril10&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_67f7bb0f5e1ada23e28ff8c9&l=en-US&s=1NyJCLfWN8ISwHD_HOVv-SS-7Q0%3D)
---
**Getting Things Done for Michigan** _I am proud to announce that as a first-term Member of Congress, I was chosen to be the Chairman of the House Veterans Affairs Subcommittee on Technology Modernization.__As the leader of that committee, I was proud to have my very first bill pass the House this week, the Clear Communication for Veterans Claims Act ([H.R. 10](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=67f7dd685e1ada23e2975ef1&u=https%3A%2F%2Fbarrett.house.gov%2Fsites%2Fevo-subsites%2Fbarrett.house.gov%2Ffiles%2Fevo-media-document%2Fbills-119hr1039ih.pdf&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_67f7bb0f5e1ada23e28ff8c9&l=en-US&s=CRdwWA_JmyId3M53CPMTRw01Cl8%3D)). This legislation will require the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to make significant improvements to its overly complicated claims process and streamline communications with veterans. This legislation is long overdue.__I was also proud to have VA Secretary Doug Collins come to the 7th District and tour two of our local VA clinics, and assuring our veterans that the reforms being implemented by the Trump Administration will make the VA more responsive to their needs with no cuts in benefits.__And finally, I was incredibly proud to play a role in securing the release of two Michiganders from a Mexican maximum security prison last week._
---
| [](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=67f7dd685e1ada23e2975ef1&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.detroitnews.com%2Fstory%2Fnews%2Fpolitics%2F2025%2F04%2F03%2Fakeo-couple-returning-to-michigan-after-released-from-mexican-prison%2F82808324007%2F%3Futm_campaign%3Ddailybriefing-greeting%26utm_content%3Dpdtn-e-nletter65%26utm_medium%3Demail%26utm_source%3Ddetroitnews-dailybriefing-strada%26utm_term%3DContent%2520List%2520-%2520Stacking%2520-%2520optimized&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_67f7bb0f5e1ada23e28ff8c9&l=en-US&s=3LddY2utQzLE69yJwSp_9gY4y-Y%3D)
---
[_ _**DONATE** _ _](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=67f7dd685e1ada23e2975ef1&u=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.winred.com%2Ftom-barrett-for-congress%2Fapril10&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_67f7bb0f5e1ada23e28ff8c9&l=en-US&s=1NyJCLfWN8ISwHD_HOVv-SS-7Q0%3D)
---
_Paul and Christy Akeo were arrested by Mexican authorities upon arriving in Cancun on March 4th and spent more than four weeks in a maximum security prison over a dispute with a time-share resort. When I learned of their plight, I reached out to the State Department and the White House to begin the process of negotiating their release.__Last Wednesday, I flew to Mexico, met with the Akeos in prison, then met with the President of the regional Supreme Court, and then talked with the resort owner. We were able to secure their release and bring them home last Thursday night.__This week, Charlie LeDuff invited me on to his podcast to discuss the operation. You can[watch it here](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=67f7dd685e1ada23e2975ef1&u=https%3A%2F%2Fyoutu.be%2FTSEmEyaTu3g&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_67f7bb0f5e1ada23e28ff8c9&l=en-US&s=tva09q7_9L8Um1E5o0WfBc9da4g%3D)._

clean_body: Thank you for your help | | | | Huge Q1 Fundraising Report The first quarter fundraising deadline has passed and I am proud to announce thatwe raised more than $800,000 \- a monster haul thanks to supporters like you.Washington Democrats are working feverishly to recruit my opponent and this fundraising accomplishment will make it that much harder.Thank you! Democrats are Coming for Me This week, the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee announced that I am one of their top targets for 2026... | But that’s not all. has me listed as one of 13 Republican-held seats that are now “Toss Ups.” The moved MI-7 from a +2 Republican seat to “Even.” The progressive has named me a top target. Getting Things Done for Michigan** I am proud to announce that as a first-term Member of Congress, I was chosen to be the Chairman of the House Veterans Affairs Subcommittee on Technology Modernization.As the leader of that committee, I was proud to have my very first bill pass the House this week, the Clear Communication for Veterans Claims Act (). This legislation will require the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to make significant improvements to its overly complicated claims process and streamline communications with veterans. This legislation is long overdue.I was also proud to have VA Secretary Doug Collins come to the 7th District and tour two of our local VA clinics, and assuring our veterans that the reforms being implemented by the Trump Administration will make the VA more responsive to their needs with no cuts in benefits.And finally, I was incredibly proud to play a role in securing the release of two Michiganders from a Mexican maximum security prison last week. | Paul and Christy Akeo were arrested by Mexican authorities upon arriving in Cancun on March 4th and spent more than four weeks in a maximum security prison over a dispute with a time-share resort. When I learned of their plight, I reached out to the State Department and the White House to begin the process of negotiating their release.Last Wednesday, I flew to Mexico, met with the Akeos in prison, then met with the President of the regional Supreme Court, and then talked with the resort owner. We were able to secure their release and bring them home last Thursday night.This week, Charlie LeDuff invited me on to his podcast to discuss the operation. name: Tom Fitton
subject: I Don’t Know How Else to Put This…
body:
Thanks to you, we are winning huge victories for transparency in government and for the rule of law...

| | |
---
|
---
|
---
| Dear Fellow American,

Thanks to our supporters across the country, we are winning huge victories for transparency in government and for the rule of law...
**But we cannot let up now ...and [we urgently need your help today](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73307&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU1heSZ1dG1fdGVybT1Ob24tRG9ub3Jz&hash=aafc90fdace25a5a1871d30fa9a66413e29290f69056683c8a490d5fbaf2a4d2)!**
| [Contribute](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73307&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU1heSZ1dG1fdGVybT1Ob24tRG9ub3Jz&hash=aafc90fdace25a5a1871d30fa9a66413e29290f69056683c8a490d5fbaf2a4d2)
---

* Our lawsuits are helping **President Trump** uncover financial corruption at key government agencies like the US Agency for International Development **(USAID)** ... _but our investigations are just scratching the surface when it comes misappropriation of taxpayer funds_ ,
* A federal appeals court ruled in our favor and in a historic ruling "laid down the law" that Mississippi cannot count ballots that arrive **after** Election Day, but we are _still fighting_ this battle against two other states,

* Last week we learned that over **FIVE MILLION** ineligible voter names have been removed from voter rolls across the country as a result of Judicial Watch lawsuits...eliminating **FIVE MILLION** opportunities for voter fraud...but we are _still in court_ against three states over their "dirty" voter rolls, and we will be suing _another_ state soon,

* And we _keep fighting_ in the courts against reparations and other ugly and unconstitutional woke programs...

The financial pressures on us are enormous...we are carrying nearly 200 active lawsuits...and [that is why I ask you to give us your support today.](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73307&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU1heSZ1dG1fdGVybT1Ob24tRG9ub3Jz&hash=aafc90fdace25a5a1871d30fa9a66413e29290f69056683c8a490d5fbaf2a4d2)

[Contribute](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73307&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU1heSZ1dG1fdGVybT1Ob24tRG9ub3Jz&hash=aafc90fdace25a5a1871d30fa9a66413e29290f69056683c8a490d5fbaf2a4d2)
---

As a conservative, I am sure you want us to succeed in our mission and that is why I ask you to support us financially with [a generous second-quarter gift](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73307&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU1heSZ1dG1fdGVybT1Ob24tRG9ub3Jz&hash=aafc90fdace25a5a1871d30fa9a66413e29290f69056683c8a490d5fbaf2a4d2).

And thank you in advance for your generosity!

Thomas Fitton
President

[Contribute](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=73307&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU1heSZ1dG1fdGVybT1Ob24tRG9ub3Jz&hash=aafc90fdace25a5a1871d30fa9a66413e29290f69056683c8a490d5fbaf2a4d2)

subject: Clarence Thomas sends a heartwarming message to every Catholic and every patriot.

body:
Please take a minute to read his message.

| | | | [](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zcs3nmshl12ls7eam7dbgqgns152pbfdcbkjahcg_rp22sh2s8i6eob1cpi64pb6cph2bs)

**“Do not hide your faith and your beliefs. Especially in this world that seems to have gone mad with political correctness.”**
**“I admit to being unapologetically Catholic and unapologetically patriotic.”**
\- Justice Clarence Thomas.\-------------------------Thank you for reading Justice Thomas’ message.
**The sad truth is, even with President Trump in office, the far-left is still waging war against America and against religion.**
And without America-First men of faith like Clarence Thomas to defend us, they’re sure to win.
As you’re reading this message, the Democrats are calling for the impeachment of Justice Clarence Thomas.
**Their petition to have him removed from the Supreme Court bench has nearly 1.4 MILLION SIGNATURES.**
If Justice Thomas goes, then there will be nothing stopping the Democrats from going after the America-First Agenda, our religious freedom, and President Trump.
That’s why it’s so important **_right here_ , _right now_ ,** to sign our counter petition and stand with Clarence Thomas.
[2,000,000 million signatures are needed by midnight to support his seat on the Supreme Court.](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zqddhujca87nslf4nl9f3dvspcgkkaasq5qlspmo_rp22sh2s8i6eob1cpi64pb6cph2bs)

Please - it only takes a second to add your name.

name: Tina for Oregon
subject: Tina will never stop fighting to defend LGBTQ+ Oregonians
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek
body:
|
#
---
Peter — No matter what happens in Washington, D.C., Governor Kotek is holding the line for Oregon and our values. Since he returned to office, President Trump has signed several executive orders attacking diversity, equity, and inclusion efforts, restricting access to gender-affirming health care, and reinstating the military ban on transgender service members. **We can’t go backward. Tina will ALWAYS defend Oregon as a safe space for the LGBTQ+ community. ** LGBTQ+ Oregonians are part of the community fabric that makes our state such a fantastic place to call home, and during these uncertain times, we’ll never stop fighting to make sure vulnerable communities everywhere are protected. **The freedom to be yourself and to love who you love should never be in question.** Tina will always stand with her community in Oregon and around the country to defend these freedoms. We invite you to join us: **[Peter, chip in today to help our team fight for complete protections for LGBTQ+ people and defend LGBTQ+ Oregonians from the Trump administration’s attempts to roll back the clock on our most fundamental rights.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105567704/538728008/-1270421739?refcode=AU_TK_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250410_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZWQ5Y2IxZC0xZjE2LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c5XIpoWE7JZTRXYgBMkq0fPFDKKTckNUkND0qLQBISs=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $5 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105567707/538728009/-1270421739?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=AU_TK_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250410_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZWQ5Y2IxZC0xZjE2LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c5XIpoWE7JZTRXYgBMkq0fPFDKKTckNUkND0qLQBISs=)

clean_body: | Peter — No matter what happens in Washington, D.C., Governor Kotek is holding the line for Oregon and our values. Since he returned to office, President Trump has signed several executive orders attacking diversity, equity, and inclusion efforts, restricting access to gender-affirming health care, and reinstating the military ban on transgender service members. We can’t go backward. Tina will ALWAYS defend Oregon as a safe space for the LGBTQ+ community. LGBTQ+ Oregonians are part of the community fabric that makes our state such a fantastic place to call home, and during these uncertain times, we’ll never stop fighting to make sure vulnerable communities everywhere are protected. The freedom to be yourself and to love who you love should never be in question. Tina will always stand with her community in Oregon and around the country to defend these freedoms. We invite you to join us: = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Rest assured, Tina will continue to push back on anything that undermines protections for LGBTQ+ Oregonians. #### **Frozen Odds -**
**Alaska — vast, untamed, and unpredictable. Beneath its snow-covered peaks and icy rivers lies a political battlefield unlike any other. This is the story they didn’t want you to know.****Powerful names — Lisa Murkowski, Hansjörg Wyss, Arabella Advisors, and George Soros — echo through the corridors of influence, shaping the future of the Last Frontier. From the manipulation of Ranked Choice Voting to the backdoor funding of initiatives like the Minimum Wage Increase, Alaska has become a testing ground — a proving ground — for tactics designed to tilt democracy itself.****Groups like Salmon State, Outdoor Alliance, and the Alaska Venture Fund, Alaskans For Better Elections, No on 2, etc. have operated quietly, weaving a web of influence across the state's political landscape. What seemed like grassroots efforts were anything but — and the connections go far deeper than anyone imagined.****This isn't just about Alaska. It’s a warning to the rest of America.****Help bring this story to life, flying experts to Alaska, traveling the state for interviews. We have already raised nearly 50% what we expected for the entire film to cost.****A huge Thank You,****Phil Izon**

#### **2025:**
**WV - Potential Ban****TX - Potential Ban****WY - Potential Ban****AK - Repeal Petition****ME - Repeal Petition in Process****NC - Potential Ban**

body:
This is huge.
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=1585%2E791427%2EWdSk5I)

Friend — This is huge!
**Today Ilhan Omar is officially launching her campaign for reelection to Congress in Minnesota’s 5th district!**
**[If you’re ready to take on the corporate interests, the far-right, and the entire political establishment to reelect Ilhan Omar and fight for our progressive values, rush a contribution to our campaign >>**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-april25.zbfFse&refcode=em250410-1-f&t=2&akid=1585%2E791427%2EWdSk5I)
**[DONATE $3](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-april25.zbfFse&amount=3&refcode=em250410-1-f&t=3&akid=1585%2E791427%2EWdSk5I)**
Since her first election to Congress in 2018, Ilhan has been a fierce and consistent fighter on the issues we care about, from tackling the climate crisis, to ensuring healthcare for all, to achieving a true ceasefire in Gaza.
Now, she is helping to lead the charge to hold Donald Trump’s administration accountable and fight back against the billionaire oligarchs who seek to take from the working class for their own benefit.
But the truth is, winning reelection has never been easy. Millions of dollars have been spent to unseat Ilhan. We have gone up against attacks from AIPAC, far-right special interests, conservative Democrats, and Donald Trump himself.
And each time we were victorious because we had a committed grassroots movement behind our campaign — a movement of folks doing what they can, when they can, to win over voters and fight for the progressive vision of the future that Ilhan and all of us share.
That’s why it’s crucial that we make today a HUGE day for our fundraising efforts. We want to show everyone who is watching this campaign that Ilhan is not just ready to compete, but ready to _**win**_ once again. We want to scare off the competition before this race even gets started.
So please.
**[Make a contribution of $3 or whatever you can afford today as we kick off this campaign for reelection. This is a big moment for Ilhan, and it would mean the world to her if you had her back today.**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-april25.zbfFse&refcode=em250410-1-f&t=4&akid=1585%2E791427%2EWdSk5I)

We’re excited to start this new campaign and to reelect Ilhan once again, and we hope you are too! We’re counting on this grassroots team to be there every step of the way.
In solidarity,
Team Ilhan Omar

clean_body: This is huge. Friend — This is huge! Today Ilhan Omar is officially launching her campaign for reelection to Congress in Minnesota’s 5th district! Since her first election to Congress in 2018, Ilhan has been a fierce and consistent fighter on the issues we care about, from tackling the climate crisis, to ensuring healthcare for all, to achieving a true ceasefire in Gaza. Now, she is helping to lead the charge to hold Donald Trump’s administration accountable and fight back against the billionaire oligarchs who seek to take from the working class for their own benefit. But the truth is, winning reelection has never been easy. Millions of dollars have been spent to unseat Ilhan. We have gone up against attacks from AIPAC, far-right special interests, conservative Democrats, and Donald Trump himself. And each time we were victorious because we had a committed grassroots movement behind our campaign — a movement of folks doing what they can, when they can, to win over voters and fight for the progressive vision of the future that Ilhan and all of us share. That’s why it’s crucial that we make today a HUGE day for our fundraising efforts. We want to show everyone who is watching this campaign that Ilhan is not just ready to compete, but ready to win once again. We want to scare off the competition before this race even gets started. So please. If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your contribution will go through immediately: | | ** | | | | | | | We’re excited to start this new campaign and to reelect Ilhan once again, and we hope you are too! We’re counting on this grassroots team to be there every step of the way. In solidarity, Team Ilhan Omar PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS

name: Gage Hoekstra
subject: Indiana Republican Party Announces 2025 Leadership Team
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE
body:
| | |
---
| | **For Immediate Release:** April 10, 2025

**Contact:** Gage Hoekstra, Press Secretary, **[ghoekstra@indiana.gop](mailto:greid@indiana.gop "greid@indiana.gop")**

**Statement from the Indiana GOP on the Election of the 2025 Leadership Team** **INDIANAPOLIS (April 10, 2025)** – The Indiana Republican Party has completed its leadership elections and is proud to announce the following officers for 2025:
* **Lana Keesling** , Chairwoman
* **Jim Johnson** , Vice Chairman
* **Aaron Minnich** , Secretary
* **Erin Lucas** , Treasurer

“As a Main Street entrepreneur, I’ve learned that the best way to build a business or organization is by recruiting and hiring the best of the best, and that’s exactly what we’ve done with Chairwoman Lana Keesling, Vice Chairman Jim Johnson, Secretary Aaron Minnich, and Treasurer Erin Lucas,” **said Governor Mike Braun.** “This all-star team will build on our party’s past successes while driving us forward and staying connected to our conservative grassroots leaders as we enact our Freedom and Opportunity Agenda for Indiana.”

“I am honored to serve as Chairwoman of the Indiana Republican Party, and I’m excited to work alongside an outstanding leadership team that represents all corners of our great state,” **said Chairwoman Lana Keesling.** “Together with Jim Johnson, Aaron Minnich, and Erin Lucas, our focus is clear: modernize and strengthen our party from the ground up, improve communication and coordination with county chairs and candidates, and just as Governor Braun has shown, deliver the best customer service Hoosier Republicans have ever seen.”

**MEET THE INDIANA GOP OFFICERS ...**

**Lana Keesling** is from Fort Wayne, serving as City Clerk. She previously held an executive role at a privately held company, overseeing finance, information technology, and human resources. Keesling is an alumna of the Richard G. Lugar Excellence in Public Service Series. **Jim Johnson** is the Chair of the Montgomery County GOP, he currently works as the Director of Intergovernmental Affairs for INDOT and has previously worked as a legislative aide in the General Assembly. He is an active member and 4th District Chair of the Indiana Federation of Young Republicans. **Aaron Minnich** is a native of New Albany and now lives in Marion County. He formerly served as Assistant Secretary for the Indiana Republican Party. Minnich has also served as Vice Chairman and Treasurer of the Floyd County GOP and was Chairman of the Indiana University Southeast College Republicans. **Erin Lucas** lives in Hendricks County and served as Interim Chairwoman of the Indiana Republican Party. She has worked in non-profit development, management, and as a Congressional aide. ###
---
---
| | |
---

Learn More [https://www.wdbj7.com/2023/09/26/virginia-western-celebrates-15th-anniversary-ccap-program/] CCAP is a successful public/private partnership that pays the cost of tuition for three years at Virginia Western Community College if a qualified student does not have sufficient financial aid to pay tuition costs.
This program has helped over 4,200 students achieve higher education!
CANDIDATE SPOTLIGHT
https://karenforvirginia.com/ [https://karenforvirginia.com/] Karen Jenkins for HB-89
Donate Here [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/karenforvirginia] Karen Jenkins is a life-long resident of the City of Suffolk. Throughout her lifetime she has passionately advocated for others and currently serves on the Suffolk School Board with a proven track record and history of accomplishments. She is running for the House of Delegates for the 89th District to continue delivering results for the people of Virginia.
She is a champion for education, public safety and mental health. As your next Delegate for the 89th District, she will continue to fight and vote for meaningful policies and legislation to support the needs of the district. At the state level, she will be positioned to write and sponsor bills and laws to benefit the schools and communities in the 89th District and throughout the commonwealth.

Carilion Clinic vs. Lewis Gale Charity Softball Game - Oct. 1 Henrietta Lacks Statue Unveiling - Oct. 4
Join us for the 6th Annual Carilion Clinic vs. Lewis Gale Charity Softball Game, being held at the Home of the Salem Red Sox - Carilion Clinic Field. A dignified sculpture will commemorate the ongoing and long unrecognized contributions of Roanoke native Henrietta Lacks, who unknowingly provided the world with the HeLa cell, fueling decades of life-saving and life changing research.
Physicians, nurses and administrators will suit up for each hospital during the nine-inning contest. Proceeds will benefit the American Heart Association. Unveiling is October 4th at 11 am at Henrietta Lacks Plaza.
Admission is $5 at the gate, kids under 10 free. Gates open at 1pm and the game starts at 2pm. 410 3rd St SW, Roanoke, VA 24011

Dear Peter, OLCV’s Annual Celebration for the Environment is coming up on Friday, May 2nd! Will you join us? **[Tickets are on sale now!](https://click.everyaction.com/k/105575520/538740165/-1181493100?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImY4NjM5MmQ5LTI0MTYtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=q_vZ4RdR3t_OYKfx0arHyEcWjnAFdIdLyOMYEbaFEl8=&emci=96cf555b-6e15-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=f86392d9-2416-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=2107230)** We know that the next four years will bring many threats to our climate and environment from the federal level, but we also know from experience that now our work in Oregon is even more critical. Together we can weather the storm and continue making progress for our climate and environment in the coming years. Let’s come together to celebrate our shared accomplishments, and ensure that we have the resources we need to continue our important work of protecting Oregon’s people, places, climate, and environment.
---
[**Get your tickets**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/105575529/538740188/-1181493100?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImY4NjM5MmQ5LTI0MTYtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=q_vZ4RdR3t_OYKfx0arHyEcWjnAFdIdLyOMYEbaFEl8=&emci=96cf555b-6e15-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=f86392d9-2416-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=2107230)

You don’t want to miss this! Sincerely, April Christenson (she/her) OLCV Development Director PS: We're giving away a Grand Canyon Glamping trip! on how to enter to win. Thank you to our generous sponsors! | | | | | ---|---|--- Oregon League of Conservation Voters 1110 SE Alder Suite 220 Portland, OR 97214 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

name: GULF OF AMERICA ALERTS
subject: This shirt could be in a museum one day. Own a piece of history >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
This shirt would look good on you!

|
---
| | | **WE DID IT! 🙌**

Thanks to patriots like you, the _Gulf of Mexico_ is now officially the **Gulf of America!** And as Elon Musk said, it's a name that "has a beautiful ring to it" \- and we couldn't agree more!

---
| [](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zl9tq3hr1f9l67vqfpc03e34c74brm5bm4fj3rt0_rp22sh2s8i6eob1cpi6aoj2clh2bs)
---

**[To celebrate this historic moment, we're excited to introduce our exclusive Gulf of America t-shirt, designed specifically for America First supporters like you. And as a valued member of our team, we're offering you an exclusive 24-hour hold on this limited-edition gear.](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zgj9m2pa8c674vcdrokkc8de10nhtil732mc144g_rp22sh2s8i6eob1cpi6aoj2clh2bs "To celebrate this historic moment, we're excited to introduce our exclusive Gulf of America t-shirt, designed specifically for America First supporters like you. And as a valued member of our team, we're offering you an exclusive 24-hour hold on this limited-edition gear.")**

But don't wait - this offer expires at 11:59 PM sharp, and we can't guarantee availability after that. Elon's DOGE team is all about efficiency, and we're taking a page from their book by keeping this offer short and sweet.

---
| | | [CLAIM YOUR GULF OF AMERICA SHIRT](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zr3f12lqd2dis94eh2hvf45jmasicpq80fae9r6g_rp22sh2s8i6eob1cpi6aoj2clh2bs "CLAIM YOUR GULF OF AMERICA SHIRT")
---

This shirt is more than just a fashion statement - _it's a declaration of your pride in America's greatness_. So claim yours now and show the world what we're made of!

Don't miss out on this opportunity to own a piece of American history. **[Order now and we'll get your shirt in the mail ASAP!](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zmf6ftbrqcl35i2lmtquaursjt326190js0l3k08_rp22sh2s8i6eob1cpi6aoj2clh2bs "Order now and we'll get your shirt in the mail ASAP!")**

House Republicans
