clean_body: House Republicans just passed a budget resolution that will slash hundreds of billions from essential programs like Medicaid and SNAP. Here's the good news: | | | Peter, As Donald Trump and Elon Musk are gutting Social Security and veteran's benefits, Republicans in Congress just passed a budget resolution that will slash hundreds of billions from essential programs like Medicaid and SNAP. Why? So they can fund tax cuts for themselves and their billionaire friends. The good news: This budget is not close to being final. Budget reconciliation is a long process, leaving us time to organize members, activists, and representatives across the country to reject this budget and pass a fair one that protects working people. The better news: House Republicans passed this resolution by just two votes. They desperately need to hold onto every last bit of support that they have — and we’re heading into a congressional recess that will put these representatives face to face with their constituents. The BEST news: There are opportunities for you to make a major impact without even leaving your home! The budget resolution passed by Republicans today would be a disaster for working families. It would make it significantly harder for millions of Americans to access medical care when they’re sick or injured, put food on the table, and more. And all of it so that Elon Musk and the other self-serving billionaires in this country can hoard even more absurd amounts of money they could never spend. Over the next two weeks, members of Congress will be in their home districts for recess, and we're making sure that they hear loud and clear from their constituents: no cuts for working families and no more tax cuts for billionaires. In solidarity, Working Families Party Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). | | Sent via . To update your email address, change your name or address, or to stop receiving emails from Working Families Party and Working Families Power, please .

name: Hirono HQ
subject: Mazie’s not a typical politician
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mazie Hirono For Hawaii**
body:
|
#
---
Peter, Mazie didn’t get into politics for money, fame, or power. She didn’t come from wealth or status. English wasn’t even Mazie’s first language — she first arrived to the United States when she was eight years old with her mother, who worked low-wage jobs with no health benefits to give her daughter a better life. **Mazie is committed to this work because her mother’s perseverance inspired her to fight for justice and dignity for all people.** She fights every day for legislation that will lift children and families out of poverty, help people access healthcare, and defend crucial federal programs that we all rely on — critical Democratic priorities that deliver for the American people. She speaks out passionately against the extremism, bigotry, and hatred of Donald Trump’s administration. She’s not afraid to stand up to billionaires like Elon Musk. She will be the first to tell you that we must protect public education, put an end to Trump’s trade war, and stand by immigrant communities. That won’t always make you friendships in Washington, but like we said, Mazie isn’t in the Senate to cozy up to corporate interests or wealthy CEOs. **She’s there to fight for you.** **[Mazie is counting on the support of this grassroots team to help her stay the course and protect our shared values in the Senate. body:
Already, thousands of workers at the Social Security Administration (SSA) have been fired or forced out. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.X6XUC7u8ppFLNtMO_55SZlNseb7gEYIOhm_EWnhzEOZKmTtnV3k2h4V2x_O3H7rdW2az5rgktBwzxQ8Ml9Wgkmv85qW-UsBonL4KRa0bcNqdICU6T3NBDdNIZG7w7wEWTkGe_X2kehZNvcqr-F2nxH79MCjA6kVW7odKquZ4OUMmdKN-pmWmGSDDdKh7whlpsr-KA7I8qPfbmYr0OMWZspkE2igSIsnW9PoYdL2drHKR0OML40IEzc879Rc04lrYnCwUA8CCLdvbBVaUPP89xd8pnrZk_wXY_kxfmycs3qtBuw6mHUGV2nWON8f2Ef9rMjugPeWL7VY1iv5qBj-rW0v_HiIhRn6EnvTVWA4SRaLqohmWZjX6zN-QpCOE3DVX5D7M1t6-hQ7yJGxT9CCfwA/4fj/Hwcue6ZwS1GRZhMfFgeE-w/h0/h001.FmG3uGW43NMdWKWW0HVg8m51QL8u6mDAAyl5ARXBwfc)

Friend, Republicans and their Wall Street donors have been seeking to dismantle and privatize Social Security since its inception 85 years ago. They want to get their hands on our hard-earned benefits and gamble our retirement security on the stock market. Because we have successfully beaten back their repeated attempts to cut or undermine Social Security in Congress, now they are dismantling Social Security in a new way: through Elon Musk’s so-called “Department of Government Efficiency” (DOGE). **Already, thousands of workers at the Social Security Administration (SSA) have been fired or forced out** , with Musk claiming that thousands more will lose their jobs. This is leading to the closure of Social Security field offices, call wait times stretching hours on end, and the time it takes to process disability claims likely to stretch well beyond a year. **A new report from Senator Bernie Sanders indicates that if Elon Musk and Donald Trump successfully gut the Social Security Administration, 67,000 people could die while awaiting approval of their disability benefits.** 1 The last Social Security Commissioner, Martin O’Malley, stated that Musk’s actions, which include gaining access to people’s sensitive Social Security information, will lead to a “system collapse” within the “next 30 to 90 days.”2 **[We’re demanding Congress act to end the Trump administration’s attacks on Social Security and save the SSA before it’s too late. Add your name now to demand Trump and Musk get their hands off Social Security!](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3_fE85A4xWSon0q-5B4ZQMVgk09iuTATutqJbz5vTqWNau3r7XRaUM93zis_Vf0QB7iH_7GANZpe5F_m2yGuLgtMBHgpEw1zym8LC1AYc9Xdcvr1_NFe0rrRbi9zvmSq43Y1a437IK9RtMciBQav9FE3LPWRiQbHdkR1f7VqARHbL5GGhlbspXbU3dIkzFstJxN03RErZKyqcdhNk863-dFkr3WaFeHR6EZhC1NgnDGAIhsf60eHiTO4uA8R18gjqnTnG_WvNez5bn7F3oBCItqoPNQXTmKLbmsd_XX7Jqis737Qm7g6qKtFLZOAd0Ui-AGHUvzXJOd9gkO1XrkjEsLJW-i3Sa69tTP5tIpnnlBm0ureFCGZw5y68lLOlzkw7s/4fj/Hwcue6ZwS1GRZhMfFgeE-w/h1/h001.kpVHcIJphNGOSumWmIeBCE4cP2_DWalAv8kKGFO97E0)** [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3_fE85A4xWSon0q-5B4ZQMVgk09iuTATutqJbz5vTqWNQSnGzQe794UsnfKrI4W3hZUvHvLx1pRAZItRV2f-cM49E_cIuZdeVHiyMpYENz7mrSzU_Uly_yTg5T2kiPmPFLGF37OJFj4YHl6Qd7Lj8b7jxQZf1hF3a1VN6U4Rk1AUJbRCIBTBNsvlWEB7FrkWn5OUuLXieLStpz-kdOruj0QlTpeWJEQWJ61G6CIbTt3brD41wZDShGpYT11gynqMbmiLXBlK9V3g8fho97hjncXwYzfgFQnZG4Ei989gI9l7IVnPJbpaPE5HMp0Xv2C9CIEHK_SjSV0ul7uhvT04FbfNEh93xt7lEGJ_1EO-DfSqDsoUEVze17CQgQRBGdrAU0/4fj/Hwcue6ZwS1GRZhMfFgeE-w/h2/h001.zZyOzd5KoeCgxJ-zHIZyomKI9lTTNODnSUKsTJXuDL0) | [TAKE ACTION](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3_fE85A4xWSon0q-5B4ZQMVgk09iuTATutqJbz5vTqWNYE6ohe6r-AuP71BgYzQ9AUT-e_-6gVlM_7TRgI9ZIanMnrWWYqowuHML7RDYdJ8_QMDkmGaLeKr0wZ2k_WgPglhJ13Yom3Xr08ZMabjsiY730RBVs-oFemaQcrfGCvxtbNnSQF0T1HWtviAse1Z_cu1R7teuLezNrPl-gYZeE-42HFoBU-KHjJpAkfa8FswE3IkJU2IE9PmHwuMbFZKSIYvc3pY_mR7O8JK0vRnJD52jI87ZzKF2qzth4iAzxJP8MV_cDnIUdUHbZd3eE0i1wIetdpVWPxFXZuD5LmvAEY_xJnpbA-pv6jUPkNTUDhTHjlEXe6EnGcA4vJXRpWzvSY/4fj/Hwcue6ZwS1GRZhMfFgeE-w/h3/h001.SofkPEK8kGDmYZCaa-2MFv1rV5ajaHLo1e1iknXC_lg)

Last Saturday, millions of people made our voices heard at Hands Off! rallies and protests in towns and cities across the country. We demanded Trump and Musk get their hands off Social Security, Medicare, Medicaid, veterans’ benefits, nutrition benefits, public education, voting rights, and our very democracy.
We won't let billionaires steal from seniors and people with disabilities. I joined the workers’ union that represents workers at the Social Security Administration―American Federation of Government Employees (AFGE)―at a rally in Michigan last Friday to defend SSA and federal workers.
[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3_fE85A4xWSon0q-5B4ZQMVgk09iuTATutqJbz5vTqWNeq05S45GlVZOs8lf-I_qzw_i8CkGb9loevd6fe9Hskx75OeQAkBSHm3N5nDNpD2FFLseLp0RN6eMXPmw1Ss3wvcQIG8g1N18emL4PKWWzqfpsC_7osSrmYszn5xUGj66btx0IpOJvIhRNY7yzfEFzi0pcPNewafgC3pRdTRma6J85__WOkqaWeffDctHgmiF8TA5q57iLsz9jBdtjkAnOcaFYvKRN_KuPwMFC2auy8S5j4x5GNzeir6RTgFauOaq73DqwwxXJ_oT2x-XVKVudoyCGFmS5UoEdXFaLn4FxudEqOdkhwI-0aN8burDBJ84KCNkKlwgd7DIo-n3rl_rnI/4fj/Hwcue6ZwS1GRZhMfFgeE-w/h4/h001.bnkfRMfYdg10A1pEiJzgiZbsBAhr4FxZM7WViHCtyGE)
Right now, more than 4 million Americans turn 65 each year. We need to be ensuring access to our lifesaving earned Social Security benefits, not putting up barriers between us and the benefits that we’ve paid into over a lifetime of work.
**[Add your name now to demand Congress act to defend Social Security from Elon Musk and Donald Trump.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3_fE85A4xWSon0q-5B4ZQMVgk09iuTATutqJbz5vTqWNVJ45YDvu0_z1_FD31isv4ticYthEtcRhp_ZFqRaPUQXa6rY-W9t90SpTtB7y0ezFVyEIrVdEFy3LabnfPvFGvC50cEB_Oer-58VLlonKi27jk3Y8j_tWuxvqBX34KIEX5vVmhxgllXRomCIk61nWki-vbFNbjEtR9sxMX9MOr0hYFe_bpwctonHKOXwyJCY47hEEoZyiyLaKTJjDIckxEGP35LLBV0bcaacO3gT6bufaEnOwxrMMlGLIEQyxRWGiSVuBhbrJvIXwWhlO7PN2nCPs6CVxBVroACu0ZDxhQluSlITwRZ6gIkXMawpOILK_ROJG05Gc9Hsg8WFHjqwFrA/4fj/Hwcue6ZwS1GRZhMfFgeE-w/h5/h001.fBMBfAI8giT7KbdYfbHTrDRh1qWlESytNTFeT2UytCg)**
Social Security lifts 27 million Americans out of poverty each year. It is the most beloved social insurance program in our country’s history. We will not let billionaires dismantle it and hand our hard-earned benefits over to Wall Street.
In solidarity,
Rashida

clean_body: Already, thousands of workers at the Social Security Administration (SSA) have been fired or forced out. | | | Friend, Republicans and their Wall Street donors have been seeking to dismantle and privatize Social Security since its inception 85 years ago. They want to get their hands on our hard-earned benefits and gamble our retirement security on the stock market. Because we have successfully beaten back their repeated attempts to cut or undermine Social Security in Congress, now they are dismantling Social Security in a new way: through Elon Musk’s so-called “Department of Government Efficiency” (DOGE). Already, thousands of workers at the Social Security Administration (SSA) have been fired or forced out , with Musk claiming that thousands more will lose their jobs. This is leading to the closure of Social Security field offices, call wait times stretching hours on end, and the time it takes to process disability claims likely to stretch well beyond a year. A new report from Senator Bernie Sanders indicates that if Elon Musk and Donald Trump successfully gut the Social Security Administration, 67,000 people could die while awaiting approval of their disability benefits. 1 The last Social Security Commissioner, Martin O’Malley, stated that Musk’s actions, which include gaining access to people’s sensitive Social Security information, will lead to a “system collapse” within the “next 30 to 90 days.”2 | Last Saturday, millions of people made our voices heard at Hands Off! rallies and protests in towns and cities across the country. We demanded Trump and Musk get their hands off Social Security, Medicare, Medicaid, veterans’ benefits, nutrition benefits, public education, voting rights, and our very democracy. We won't let billionaires steal from seniors and people with disabilities. I joined the workers’ union that represents workers at the Social Security Administration―American Federation of Government Employees (AFGE)―at a rally in Michigan last Friday to defend SSA and federal workers. Right now, more than 4 million Americans turn 65 each year. We need to be ensuring access to our lifesaving earned Social Security benefits, not putting up barriers between us and the benefits that we’ve paid into over a lifetime of work. Social Security lifts 27 million Americans out of poverty each year. It is the most beloved social insurance program in our country’s history. We will not let billionaires dismantle it and hand our hard-earned benefits over to Wall Street. subject: April 10th, 2025 Legislative Update
body:
Dear Neighbors, We are halfway through the session and coming up on the Work Session deadline on April 9th.
---
| | | [](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fcontent.govdelivery.com%2Faccounts%2FORLEG%2Fbulletins%2F3db0d93/1/0101019621b08f93-1b5f6dbb-d3ec-451e-b1b9-d852813fc118-000000/EPy6hMCvO04B_Qazk_ZmdgbZmwr2YwM61mzgAaRo-og=400)
---|---
---
Replies to this message are sent to an unmonitored mailbox.
To contact me, please click here: [Rep.DarinHarbick@oregonlegislature.gov](mailto:Rep.DarinHarbick@oregonlegislature.gov) |
* * *
---
Dear Neighbors,
We are halfway through the session and coming up on the Work Session deadline on April 9th. I wanted to update you on what I've been working on and what is coming up in the Legislature. I have enjoyed meeting everyone that has come by the office. It's been an honor to serve as the Representative from House District 12.
---
* * *
---
# [HB 3564](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FHB3564/1/0101019621b08f93-1b5f6dbb-d3ec-451e-b1b9-d852813fc118-000000/UdGLFkanKDB5M_JOqube0pCzEU-nKD-5DTgWPCRlWHo=400)
One of the bills I drafted, HB 3564 has made it out of committee and will be coming up on the House Floor for a vote. This bill helps everyday Oregonians seek a correction when they have been defamed by the media. News outlets are very powerful because they have large platforms and an appearance of being an authority on truthful news. One false story from a bad actor posing as a journalist has the potential to ruin someone's reputation.
Under current law, in order to bring a defamation claim against a publisher or broadcaster, a plaintiff needs to send the demand for correction or retraction within 20 days after the defamed person receives actual knowledge of the defamatory statement. I felt that timeframe of 20 days was too short. Trying to find an attorney who specializes in defamation cases is very difficult in Oregon. After finding an attorney more time is needed to research what to put in that letter. Once the letter is finalized it then needs to be sent by certified mail to the editor. All of those steps need to be completed in the 20-day window. HB 3564 seeks to increase that timeline to 40 days.
HB 3564 also seeks to modernize the statutes around correcting defamatory statements. The majority of Oregonians now consume their news digitally. HB 3564 clarifies that the retraction statute also applies to the digital copy published on the website. It makes the publishers (if they have agreed to issue a correction) go back to the original article that was published online and either delete the defamatory statements or put a link to the correction on the article. Current law does not require them to do this. This is very important because online archived material is permanent, and online articles form the basis for information created via artificial intelligence. If a publisher has issued a correction, it is not an overly burdensome request to have them go back and edit an online article to reflect that correction.
---
* * *
---
# Oregon’s New Tax and Fee Proposal: A Massive Burden on Rural Families and Small Towns
* * *
---
---
Democrats in the House and Senate have unveiled a multi-billion dollar tax-and-spend package aimed at the Oregon Department of Transportation (ODOT), a plan that’s only going to make it harder for Oregonians to keep more of their hard earned paychecks. This proposal, while promising improvements, comes at a time when families across Oregon are already struggling to make ends meet.
Here’s what this plan means for everyday Oregonians:
* **Gas tax increase by $0.20** (a 50% hike)—driving up fuel costs for everyone, especially rural families who rely on longer commutes.
* **Car registration fees** will jump by $66—an added burden when the cost of living already feels overwhelming.
* **DMV and title fees** increase by $90—increasing yet another fee on the everyday transactions that rural communities can’t avoid.
* **Truckers:** those who help transport goods across Oregon—will see a **16.9% increase** in the weight-mile tax, raising prices across the board for everything from groceries to construction materials.
* **New car tax: a****1% “one-time system use fee”** on new cars, making it even more expensive to replace aging vehicles.
* **Car sales tax** increases by 60%—raising the cost of buying vehicles that are often essential in rural areas.
* **Payroll taxes** increase by 125%—a staggering hike that could lead to fewer jobs, fewer opportunities, and fewer chances to make a living.
* **Tire tax** : a new **3% tax** on tires—punishing rural drivers who depend on their vehicles to work, to get to town, and to keep things moving.
* A **new EV road use charge**
* **Delivery vehicle charge:** forcing businesses and communities to pay even more for deliveries, raising the costs of goods and services.
* **Bike tax** increase by **$9.50**

This package asks rural Oregonians to pay billions more, while getting less in return. It’s time to ask: whom is this plan really helping? Certainly not the working-class people across Oregon, especially in rural areas where we already carry the weight of high transportation costs. ODOT has failed to deliver results, and now they’re asking us to pay for their mistakes.
* * *
---
# Businesses Leaving Oregon
A recent University of Oregon [report](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.oregon.gov%2Fbiz%2FPublications%2FBiz_Recruitment.pdf/1/0101019621b08f93-1b5f6dbb-d3ec-451e-b1b9-d852813fc118-000000/aeWIuNJFK2chBooh8kacfd-vQ_Y5ym3osYrwZx1QH4o=400) shows out-of-state businesses are actively recruiting Oregon workers, using tax incentives as “by far the most commonly offered” incentive to leave the state.
Researchers surveyed nearly 400 traded sector businesses and conducted more than 30 interviews. The results show:
* 24% of businesses reported being approached by recruiting agencies outside Oregon.
* 68% of businesses contacted by a recruiting agency reported moving or expanding outside Oregon.
* 60% said external recruitment efforts have increased in the past three years.

The fact that Oregonians are being enticed to leave our state is an indictment on the tax structure and regulatory environment that is forcing businesses to plan to grow beyond state lines.
The researchers recommended that the Governor’s office make economic development a more prominent priority, writing, “While we understand the rationale and urgency behind the Governor’s key priorities, we argue that economic development (thought of in terms prosperity for all Oregonians) is deeply linked with the state’s housing affordability and homelessness crises.”
This report concluded what House Republicans have been saying all along: To strengthen our economy, our state must support businesses by improving incentives and cutting taxes and regulatory burdens.
* * *
---
# A Letter to the Speaker
My colleagues and I penned a letter to the Speaker of the House, Julie Fahey, requesting fair hearings, work sessions, and Floor votes for our top priority bills that will help you and your family keep more of what you earn.
In our letter to Speaker Fahey, we asked for three bills to be prioritized to help provide desperate relief to hard-working Oregonians like you. My Republican colleagues and I are united and prepared to use every tool to advance these bills and address Oregon’s affordability crisis.
[**HB 3753**](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FHB3753/1/0101019621b08f93-1b5f6dbb-d3ec-451e-b1b9-d852813fc118-000000/SwbUXjTqaiWQ3Z4ovfkUZksZ9fUWrlifTaF4uGoalgA=400)**–** Helps working families and tax-burdened Oregonians keep more of their paycheck by nearly tripling the standard deduction allowed for personal income taxpayers.
[**HB 3914**](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FHB3914/1/0101019621b08f93-1b5f6dbb-d3ec-451e-b1b9-d852813fc118-000000/1jO9hao8K--zjf5h_bmBZc5SOplv-E8ZgxTwOfc17G0=400)**–** Creates a personal income tax subtraction for tips received by thousands of Oregonians in the hospitality and service industries, helping them keep more money in their pockets as they face inflation and rising costs.
[**HB 2234**](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FHB2234/1/0101019621b08f93-1b5f6dbb-d3ec-451e-b1b9-d852813fc118-000000/wA-9ahfM0wD3xyi0RrLZeM87C-Uo09CBo5oo-iR4EJE=400)**–** Creates a personal income tax subtraction for overtime pay received by Oregonians who work extra hours to support their families. No tax on overtime is a great way to give working Oregonians real, tangible relief from rising costs.
Oregonians continue to struggle to pay the bills and afford basic necessities like groceries, utilities, gas, and rent. [Oregon ranks as the 10th most expensive state](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fgcc02.safelinks.protection.outlook.com%2F%3Furl=https%253A%252F%252Fwww.forbes.com%252Fadvisor%252Fmortgages%252Fcost-of-living-by-state%252F%26data=05%257C02%257CSam.Herscovitz%2540oregonlegislature.gov%257C474060b24f04412ddeed08dd673fcc52%257C489a9c84574a48c7b72a2450511334cc%257C1%257C0%257C638780245891466599%257CUnknown%257CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%253D%253D%257C0%257C%257C%257C%26sdata=fiJEBZz9XnzCoRhvDVaxuFejHgxxrbqiQgmcmEeB7G4%253D%26reserved=0/1/0101019621b08f93-1b5f6dbb-d3ec-451e-b1b9-d852813fc118-000000/aQ6pb3k5_qeWQogVwwPHV3VzxjuafkKGYkBKw6qyio0=400) to live in and it has the [5th highest individual income tax rate](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Ftaxfoundation.org%2Fdata%2Fall%2Fstate%2Fstate-income-tax-rates%2F/1/0101019621b08f93-1b5f6dbb-d3ec-451e-b1b9-d852813fc118-000000/gUEEZQ-jAMzgoCpKJllBHqgU5WiWgUcdxFTZCe_3FNw=400). Only California, Hawaii, New York and New Jersey have higher individual income tax rates than we do.
A recent [January poll](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fb8cc96c3-4e55-4c21-9e2b-107dbb2eac36.usrfiles.com%2Fugd%2Fb8cc96_38da181920a749779fc0501b1c565893.pdf/1/0101019621b08f93-1b5f6dbb-d3ec-451e-b1b9-d852813fc118-000000/FO39drVFlvDOSteSNrwLMFwQB9ds7HRK3nIoH90B-hs=400) revealed that 79% of Oregonians favor cutting taxes to make Oregon more affordable and 70% favor eliminating state income taxes on tips and overtime pay. The same poll showed 56% of Oregonians say that taxes are too high.
I promise to continue to fight for policies that lower the cost of living and work to make Oregon a more affordable place for you and your family.
---
---
* * *
---
As we come up on the last half of the 2025 long session, I will keep fighting for my hard-working constituents in rural East Lane County.
Best regards,
Representative Darin Harbick
House District 12
**Capitol Phone:** 503-986-1412
**Capitol Address:** 900 Court St. NE, H-376, Salem, Oregon 97301
**Email:** [Rep.DarinHarbick@oregonlegislature.gov](mailto:Rep.DarinHarbick@oregonlegislature.gov)
**Website:**[https://www.oregonlegislature.gov/harbick](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fharbick/1/0101019621b08f93-1b5f6dbb-d3ec-451e-b1b9-d852813fc118-000000/6a_Ffy_CiqgyZ-KWdK0JxdH6ILL0BW9Jtt2DXCCViCU=400)

clean_body: Dear Neighbors, We are halfway through the session and coming up on the Work Session deadline on April 9th. | | | ---|--- Replies to this message are sent to an unmonitored mailbox. To contact me, please click here: mailto:Rep.DarinHarbick@oregonlegislature.gov | Dear Neighbors, We are halfway through the session and coming up on the Work Session deadline on April 9th. I wanted to update you on what I've been working on and what is coming up in the Legislature. I have enjoyed meeting everyone that has come by the office. It's been an honor to serve as the Representative from House District 12. One of the bills I drafted, HB 3564 has made it out of committee and will be coming up on the House Floor for a vote. This bill helps everyday Oregonians seek a correction when they have been defamed by the media. News outlets are very powerful because they have large platforms and an appearance of being an authority on truthful news. One false story from a bad actor posing as a journalist has the potential to ruin someone's reputation. Under current law, in order to bring a defamation claim against a publisher or broadcaster, a plaintiff needs to send the demand for correction or retraction within 20 days after the defamed person receives actual knowledge of the defamatory statement. I felt that timeframe of 20 days was too short. Trying to find an attorney who specializes in defamation cases is very difficult in Oregon. After finding an attorney more time is needed to research what to put in that letter. Once the letter is finalized it then needs to be sent by certified mail to the editor. All of those steps need to be completed in the 20-day window. HB 3564 seeks to increase that timeline to 40 days. HB 3564 also seeks to modernize the statutes around correcting defamatory statements. The majority of Oregonians now consume their news digitally. HB 3564 clarifies that the retraction statute also applies to the digital copy published on the website. It makes the publishers (if they have agreed to issue a correction) go back to the original article that was published online and either delete the defamatory statements or put a link to the correction on the article. Current law does not require them to do this. This is very important because online archived material is permanent, and online articles form the basis for information created via artificial intelligence. If a publisher has issued a correction, it is not an overly burdensome request to have them go back and edit an online article to reflect that correction. Oregon’s New Tax and Fee Proposal: A Massive Burden on Rural Families and Small Towns Democrats in the House and Senate have unveiled a multi-billion dollar tax-and-spend package aimed at the Oregon Department of Transportation (ODOT), a plan that’s only going to make it harder for Oregonians to keep more of their hard earned paychecks. This proposal, while promising improvements, comes at a time when families across Oregon are already struggling to make ends meet. Here’s what this plan means for everyday Oregonians: Gas tax increase by $0.20 (a 50% hike)—driving up fuel costs for everyone, especially rural families who rely on longer commutes. Car registration fees will jump by $66—an added burden when the cost of living already feels overwhelming. DMV and title fees increase by $90—increasing yet another fee on the everyday transactions that rural communities can’t avoid. Truckers: those who help transport goods across Oregon—will see a 16.9% increase in the weight-mile tax, raising prices across the board for everything from groceries to construction materials. New car tax: a**1% “one-time system use fee” on new cars, making it even more expensive to replace aging vehicles. Car sales tax increases by 60%—raising the cost of buying vehicles that are often essential in rural areas. Payroll taxes increase by 125%—a staggering hike that could lead to fewer jobs, fewer opportunities, and fewer chances to make a living. Tire tax : a new 3% tax on tires—punishing rural drivers who depend on their vehicles to work, to get to town, and to keep things moving. A new EV road use charge Delivery vehicle charge: forcing businesses and communities to pay even more for deliveries, raising the costs of goods and services. Bike tax increase by $9.50 This package asks rural Oregonians to pay billions more, while getting less in return. It’s time to ask: whom is this plan really helping? Certainly not the working-class people across Oregon, especially in rural areas where we already carry the weight of high transportation costs. ODOT has failed to deliver results, and now they’re asking us to pay for their mistakes. Businesses Leaving Oregon A recent University of Oregon shows out-of-state businesses are actively recruiting Oregon workers, using tax incentives as “by far the most commonly offered” incentive to leave the state. Researchers surveyed nearly 400 traded sector businesses and conducted more than 30 interviews. The results show: 24% of businesses reported being approached by recruiting agencies outside Oregon. 68% of businesses contacted by a recruiting agency reported moving or expanding outside Oregon. 60% said external recruitment efforts have increased in the past three years. The fact that Oregonians are being enticed to leave our state is an indictment on the tax structure and regulatory environment that is forcing businesses to plan to grow beyond state lines. The researchers recommended that the Governor’s office make economic development a more prominent priority, writing, “While we understand the rationale and urgency behind the Governor’s key priorities, we argue that economic development (thought of in terms prosperity for all Oregonians) is deeply linked with the state’s housing affordability and homelessness crises.” This report concluded what House Republicans have been saying all along: To strengthen our economy, our state must support businesses by improving incentives and cutting taxes and regulatory burdens. A Letter to the Speaker My colleagues and I penned a letter to the Speaker of the House, Julie Fahey, requesting fair hearings, work sessions, and Floor votes for our top priority bills that will help you and your family keep more of what you earn. In our letter to Speaker Fahey, we asked for three bills to be prioritized to help provide desperate relief to hard-working Oregonians like you. My Republican colleagues and I are united and prepared to use every tool to advance these bills and address Oregon’s affordability crisis. – Helps working families and tax-burdened Oregonians keep more of their paycheck by nearly tripling the standard deduction allowed for personal income taxpayers. – Creates a personal income tax subtraction for tips received by thousands of Oregonians in the hospitality and service industries, helping them keep more money in their pockets as they face inflation and rising costs. – Creates a personal income tax subtraction for overtime pay received by Oregonians who work extra hours to support their families. No tax on overtime is a great way to give working Oregonians real, tangible relief from rising costs. Oregonians continue to struggle to pay the bills and afford basic necessities like groceries, utilities, gas, and rent. to live in and it has the . Only California, Hawaii, New York and New Jersey have higher individual income tax rates than we do. A recent revealed that 79% of Oregonians favor cutting taxes to make Oregon more affordable and 70% favor eliminating state income taxes on tips and overtime pay. The same poll showed 56% of Oregonians say that taxes are too high. I promise to continue to fight for policies that lower the cost of living and work to make Oregon a more affordable place for you and your family. As we come up on the last half of the 2025 long session, I will keep fighting for my hard-working constituents in rural East Lane County. Best regards, Representative Darin Harbick House District 12 Capitol Phone: 503-986-1412 Capitol Address: 900 Court St. NE, H-376, Salem, Oregon 97301 Email: mailto:Rep.DarinHarbick@oregonlegislature.gov Website: SUBSCRIBER SERVICES OPTIONS: • To stop receiving this newsletter, add new newsletters, ordelete your subscription services click • For questions about subscriber services, please email Information provided may not be final and is subject to change.

name: Josh Harder
subject: Article I think you’d be interested in
body:
If helpful

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105610147/538810971/-1860252395?refcode=-header-&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDMxMTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2I2Y2M2ZTYtNTUxNi1mMDExLThiM2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=AFzyC15Vu8oS7XUC8ptcdfoaadVmtToWqv0zN89J3WE=) | Peter, I was just reading an article I thought you might find interesting. It’s about the trend of Latino voters shifting towards Trump and directly mentions my race as one of the districts that saw one of the largest swings towards Republicans in the country. [Here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105610149/538810977/-1531720934?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDMxMTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2I2Y2M2ZTYtNTUxNi1mMDExLThiM2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=AFzyC15Vu8oS7XUC8ptcdfoaadVmtToWqv0zN89J3WE=&emci=6686ae4a-2716-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=7b6cc6e6-5516-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=6455652) is the article if you want to read it for yourself. **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105610151/538810979/-1531720934?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDMxMTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2I2Y2M2ZTYtNTUxNi1mMDExLThiM2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=AFzyC15Vu8oS7XUC8ptcdfoaadVmtToWqv0zN89J3WE=&emci=6686ae4a-2716-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=7b6cc6e6-5516-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=6455652)** I thought this was super interesting because it’s something I’ve been thinking about for a few years now. Latino voters swung heavily towards Trump in the past few cycles; _however,_ here in my community, I’ve been able to continue to win their vote. This ticket splitting is vital in toss-up seats like mine. However, if Democrats keep losing at the national level, I’m in deep trouble. The fact that I was named in this article is all the proof we need. **The truth is, whichever party wins these ticket-splitters in the next election will have “a clear electoral advantage in the years ahead.”** My campaign has never taken Latino voters for granted. First off, we recognize that this isn’t a monolithic voting block. 42% of my district identifies as Hispanic or Latino; each family has a different story and different concerns. That’s why my campaign takes Latino organizing so seriously. We can’t just talk to these families right before the election, so in the past we’ve hired Latino organizers as early as possible. We need year-round organizing to prove Democrats are actually doing something to help. **But we need the resources to make these same investments this cycle so we can continue building relationships and earning the support of every single voter.** [_**So can I count on you to chip in now towards our Latino organizing program? clean_body: If helpful | = | Peter, I was just reading an article I thought you might find interesting. It’s about the trend of Latino voters shifting towards Trump and directly mentions my race as one of the districts that saw one of the largest swings towards Republicans in the country. is the article if you want to read it for yourself. I thought this was super interesting because it’s something I’ve been thinking about for a few years now. Latino voters swung heavily towards Trump in the past few cycles; however, here in my community, I’ve been able to continue to win their vote. This ticket splitting is vital in toss-up seats like mine. However, if Democrats keep losing at the national level, I’m in deep trouble. The fact that I was named in this article is all the proof we need. The truth is, whichever party wins these ticket-splitters in the next election will have “a clear electoral advantage in the years ahead.” My campaign has never taken Latino voters for granted. First off, we recognize that this isn’t a monolithic voting block. 42% of my district identifies as Hispanic or Latino; each family has a different story and different concerns. That’s why my campaign takes Latino organizing so seriously. We can’t just talk to these families right before the election, so in the past we’ve hired Latino organizers as early as possible. We need year-round organizing to prove Democrats are actually doing something to help. But we need the resources to make these same investments this cycle so we can continue building relationships and earning the support of every single voter. = If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your payment will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = ---|--- = | = ---|--- Thank you for understanding why this is so important, \- Josh *

Hi Friend,
We’re all looking for leaders to fight what Trump and his enablers are doing to the nation.
We’re all looking to take action and do something to stop the dismantling of our system of government, our economy, and our legal system.It begins here, and it starts with the essential institution that is supposed to uphold the idea of “Equal justice under law” but has been corrupted by Trump and the radical MAGA majority he put on the Supreme Court.
This is Alan Cohn from Term Limit the Court, the national campaign working with 75 members of the House and Senate to end lifetime appointments to SCOTUS and enact 18-year term limits.
Clarence Thomas and Samuel Alito believe they can replace the rule of law with their narrow-minded extreme politics because they have lifetime jobs, no oversight, no guardrails, and no binding ethics code.And they’re right!But we’re about to send them into the retirement we deserve. We’re working with Adam Schiff, Hakeem Jeffries, and others to flip the House and Senate and pass the Term Act.
Please help us back candidates for the midterms who will deliver the majority and pledge to support SCOTUS term limits.
[**https://secure.actblue.com/donate/term-limits-yes-inc-1**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105610707/538812533/-859072474?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS8xMDE4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTI3MDRjMjQtNTcxNi1mMDExLThiM2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=xShqZZomR7WyPghrf2-M70fonOmAe2clVsyxOeJA_qA=)
It’s time to get up, dust off, and make the changes we need to have the country we and our children deserve.
**[>>>Join our movement!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105610708/538812534/-859072474?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS8xMDE4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTI3MDRjMjQtNTcxNi1mMDExLThiM2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=xShqZZomR7WyPghrf2-M70fonOmAe2clVsyxOeJA_qA=)**
Alan Cohn
Term Limit the Court

* * *
The American people have lost confidence in the Supreme Court after a series of rulings that strike at the core of our freedoms. _There are term limits for a President. Term limits for many governors and state legislators. And the time has come for term limits for members of the Supreme Court._

**Now, we're counting on your help to provide the resources we need to work directly with members of Congress and their staff to support the introduction of term-limit legislation and raise awareness about this legislative solution to the growing distrust in the Supreme Court.**

**[Please pitch in what you can today to support the national campaign to create term limits for the Supreme Court.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105610709/538812536/1643639367?refcode=-footer-&amount=20&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS8xMDE4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTI3MDRjMjQtNTcxNi1mMDExLThiM2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=xShqZZomR7WyPghrf2-M70fonOmAe2clVsyxOeJA_qA=)**

Kevin Hassett is the Director of the National Economic Council, on today's show he walks us through everything that happened this week with respect to tariffs and the U.S. economy. As country after country lined-up to make a deal with President Trump, the president put a pause on all tariffs except China. President Trump has now raised the tariffs to 145% in relation to China. Essentially isolating China with the most extreme tariffs set the stage for other countries to decide how they wanted to respond. Hassett says 15 countries or more will come away with a good deal in the coming days and weeks. The turmoil in the bond market settled as the president called for the pause and new treasury bonds were very successfully auctioned off. Hassett walks us down Main St. for American consumers and small business owner as to what they can expect to pay for goods. With 2 outstanding jobs reports and a reduction in inflation, the only question yet to be answered is will China come to the table? After that our all-star panel gives their take on all the movement this week within the Trump administration. We have heard from a few different cabinet members this week, whose messaging is landing the most with Americans? Is President Trump isolating China a perfect execution of the Art of the Deal? Our panel weighs in...

body:
North Carolina is fed up with Thom Tillis. |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105610884/538812805/-1960556440?refcode=AU_GWN_FR_LAU_GEN_AC_20250410_PM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MTM0NzM0Ni01NzE2LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=79tgwRy5f9YDpGnqAmigQNAgCf6a7B6BzbDE39kSCC4=)
---
For the past several months, Wiley has traveled across North Carolina to meet with teachers, farmers, small-business owners, nurses, and other everyday North Carolinians about the future of our state. **This much is clear, Peter: North Carolina is ready for bold, new leadership.** Thom Tillis has served in the United States Senate for over 10 years — and in that time, he has _repeatedly_ sided with billionaires like Donald Trump and Elon Musk who are taking away our rights and hard-earned benefits, deadset on destroying democracy as we know it. And now, just this week, Thom Tillis had the audacity to gripe at a hearing, questioning the U.S. Trade Representative about who he can blame if Trump’s tariffs turn out to be a disaster. **Tillis needs to look in the mirror — he voted for those tariffs just last week! He has only himself to blame. Hypocrisy at its finest.** The cost of living has skyrocketed, voting has become harder, and our kids now live in a world where they have fewer rights than we did growing up. Let that sink in. **Wiley is fed up, Peter, and so are millions of North Carolinians. The same old, same old is not going to cut it.** That’s why he’s running for the Senate to offer a new vision of leadership for North Carolina — one where extremism is no longer tolerated, our rights are protected, and improving the lives of working families, not billionaires, is our top priority. **[**It’s time for North Carolina to have a leader in the United States Senate who will fight for them and their families — not take their marching orders from Donald Trump and Elon Musk. name: New Dems
subject: Demand Congress end Trump’s trade wars!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Democrat Coalition Action Fund
body:
Stand up for American workers, businesses, and families now >>

| | In less than a week after Trump’s declaration of the largest sales tax in U.S. history, **global markets have tanked, companies have laid off workers, and Americans are bracing for higher prices on everyday items.** **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105610970/538813040/530869206?refcode=NDCAF_20250410_EMPET_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjcwNGMyNC01NzE2LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KSjP9lnR4TrpzmUZ8tBWD0ZeA4jbP_Bin86rZblYoxE=&emci=a1226f88-2216-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=f2704c24-5716-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=48524772)** Trump’s 90-day tariff pause is not sufficient, we need Trump’s reckless unilateral trade strategy at a full stop. | [DEMAND CONGRESS END TRUMP’S TRADE WARS »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105610972/538813042/530869206?refcode=NDCAF_20250410_EMPET_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjcwNGMyNC01NzE2LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KSjP9lnR4TrpzmUZ8tBWD0ZeA4jbP_Bin86rZblYoxE=&emci=a1226f88-2216-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=f2704c24-5716-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=48524772)

Under the guise of protecting U.S. interests, he’s only protecting his own and those of the billionaire class.

Trump isn’t just waging war on global partners, **he’s waging war on American workers, business, and families without the consent of Congress.**

That’s why New Dems have already introduced legislation in Congress to intervene, but their MAGA colleagues won’t budge no matter how much Americans are suffering.

**[That’s why we’re asking grassroots supporters to speak up in strong opposition: Can you add your name now to demand Congress bring a decisive end to Trump’s trade wars?! »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105610973/538813045/530869206?refcode=NDCAF_20250410_EMPET_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjcwNGMyNC01NzE2LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KSjP9lnR4TrpzmUZ8tBWD0ZeA4jbP_Bin86rZblYoxE=&emci=a1226f88-2216-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=f2704c24-5716-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=48524772)**
[DEMAND CONGRESS END TRUMP’S TRADE WARS »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105610974/538813046/530869206?refcode=NDCAF_20250410_EMPET_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMjcwNGMyNC01NzE2LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KSjP9lnR4TrpzmUZ8tBWD0ZeA4jbP_Bin86rZblYoxE=&emci=a1226f88-2216-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=f2704c24-5716-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=48524772)

name: David Hogg
subject: No one is coming to save us
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
We need more young leaders stepping up — not waiting for permission.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20250410-1&t=1&akid=1964%2E587540%2EXIbp32)
Hey Peter,
The other day, I got to speak at the Women’s National Democratic Club, and we had an honest conversation about what it means to lead right now.
**Here’s the truth:**
**No one is coming to save us. No one. No president. No one. It has to be us that saves us.**
[ ](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20250410-1?refcode=em20250410-1964&amount=5&refcode2=1964_587540_XIbp32&akid=1964%2E587540%2EXIbp32)
**We’re living through a moment of real crisis:**
⚠️ Tens of thousands of federal workers laid off
⚠️ Prices and the cost of living are skyrocketing
⚠️Our healthcare system is under attack
⚠️ An administration that’s focused on cruelty and chaos than solving problems
_**So what do we do?**_
**We organize. We run. We lead.**
That’s why we started **Leaders We Deserve** — to support bold, young people who aren’t waiting around for permission to lead. We recruit and back Gen Z and Millennial candidates who’ve lived through gun violence, climate disasters, economic injustice — and who are ready to fight for a better future.
We don’t take corporate PAC money. We’re not backed by billionaires. We’re powered by the grassroots — by people like _**YOU.**_
**[So Peter, can you pitch in $5 now to power the next generation of leaders?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20250410-1?refcode=em20250410-1964&amount=5&refcode2=1964_587540_XIbp32&akid=1964%2E587540%2EXIbp32)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20250410-1?amount=5&refcode=em20250410-1964&refcode2=1964_587540_XIbp32&akid=1964%2E587540%2EXIbp32)**
This movement isn’t about one election or one moment. It’s about building the leadership we’ve been waiting for. So, let’s stop waiting and start building our future.
Thank you,
David Hogg
Leaders We Deserve
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20250410-1&t=2&refcode2=1964_587540_XIbp32&akid=1964%2E587540%2EXIbp32)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=1964%2E587540%2EXIbp32)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=1964%2E587540%2EXIbp32)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)
PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE

| | Team,

name: Kristen McDonald Rivet
subject: Trump’s budget passed – here’s what that means for MI-08:
body:
|
#
Friend, This morning, House Republicans passed Donald Trump’s budget that will give trillions to billionaires and big corporations, while putting 224,011 of our neighbors at risk of losing Medicaid coverage – including 87,412 children and 19,000 seniors. All told, the budget slashes **$880 billion** from the program. The budget also calls for a staggering **$230 billion** in cuts that will affect food assistance programs like SNAP – ripping away basic nutrition support from families just trying to get by. Folks, this is an attack on working families and the most vulnerable among us. And it comes as the President’s reckless tariffs are plunging the country into economic uncertainty and driving up costs for everyday Americans. Look, I earned the trust of Trump and Harris voters alike by promising to fight for families who work hard and play by the rules – not sell them out. Today, House Republicans sold working people out. But I won't. My job isn’t to play political games – it’s to protect the people who trusted me to fight for them. And you have my word: every day in Congress, I’ll keep fighting for you, your family, and our community. [**The best way to put a check on the White House is to flip the House blue next November, and that starts with defending Trump-won seats like ours in MI-08. Please consider chipping in**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105611456/538814800/846650059?refcode=em-4.10.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5N2ExNjA4Yi01ODE2LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jfwSKCxFhGNTHGzaM_YHVEK2ZwSHesv5FdSLoSX_iGQ=) [**$3 to my campaign today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105611457/538814801/846650059?refcode=em-4.10.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5N2ExNjA4Yi01ODE2LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jfwSKCxFhGNTHGzaM_YHVEK2ZwSHesv5FdSLoSX_iGQ=) __If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: __ | | | **[Chip in $10 Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105611458/538814802/846650059?refcode=em-4.10.25-all-a&express_lane=true&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5N2ExNjA4Yi01ODE2LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jfwSKCxFhGNTHGzaM_YHVEK2ZwSHesv5FdSLoSX_iGQ=)**

clean_body: | Friend, This morning, House Republicans passed Donald Trump’s budget that will give trillions to billionaires and big corporations, while putting 224,011 of our neighbors at risk of losing Medicaid coverage – including 87,412 children and 19,000 seniors. All told, the budget slashes $880 billion from the program. The budget also calls for a staggering $230 billion in cuts that will affect food assistance programs like SNAP – ripping away basic nutrition support from families just trying to get by. Folks, this is an attack on working families and the most vulnerable among us. And it comes as the President’s reckless tariffs are plunging the country into economic uncertainty and driving up costs for everyday Americans. Look, I earned the trust of Trump and Harris voters alike by promising to fight for families who work hard and play by the rules – not sell them out. Today, House Republicans sold working people out. But I won't. My job isn’t to play political games – it’s to protect the people who trusted me to fight for them. And you have my word: every day in Congress, I’ll keep fighting for you, your family, and our community. = = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = ** | = | | = | = | | = | =** Thank you, Kristen | We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Thank you for supporting Kristen McDonald Rivet for Congress! Kristen for Michigan P.O. Box 854 Bay City, MI 48707 United States

name: Scott Peters
subject: Prices will raise 50% for San Diegans
disclaimer_text: paid for by importers
body:
---
| | President Donald Trump proudly calls himself “Tariff Man” – and here in San Diego, we’re the ones paying the bill for his political stunts.

His reckless trade war isn’t just harming our global relationships with allies. It’s hiking prices on everything from gas to groceries to health care. And make no mistake, Peter – Trump’s 90-day pause on tariffs doesn’t change the situation. It only accentuates the air of uncertainty and unreliability Trump has created.

**In my latest _San Diego Union-Tribune_ op-ed, which was published the day before Trump’s tariff pause, I broke down how Trump’s trade war is making life exponentially harder for San Diegans, tanking the economy, and complicating our ability to work with our allies. Here’s an excerpt:** _**"Opinion: Tariffs will hammer the local, state and national economy"**_ | | [READ THE FULL OP-ED](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105611508/538815024/1614592479?refcode2=888c34af-5816-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&refcode=SP_EM_EN_90DO_250410_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzkvMS84ODA2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ODhjMzRhZi01ODE2LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hu69L-DeKvJBxO5QoqtcokEcE6Uliw2h1mG1b-AzRq4=&emci=c92f8a7f-5716-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=888c34af-5816-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=5258741)
---
_“A tariff is a tax levied on imported goods and services and paid for by importers. Trump says foreign countries pay these taxes. They do not. American companies and the American people pay for them. San Diego importers pay them.

San Diegans will see the cost of imported goods skyrocket as tariffs of up to 50 percent take effect. A leading trade organization estimates that they could cost households in San Diego and throughout the country as much as $7,600 per year.

You don’t need to take my word to understand the threat of Trump’s tariffs; we already know from experience. In 2018, President Trump instituted tariffs on Chinese goods. American consumers, not China, paid the cost for that decision to the tune of over $4 billion a month, by some estimates.” _
In essence, Peter, Trump’s trade war isn’t against China or another foreign nation – it’s against American consumers, particularly San Diego residents. And when Democrats highlight the truth about his reckless policies, as I did in this article, Trump backs down!

Peter, I’m doing everything I can to push back on Trump’s reckless trade war and protect California families and businesses. I need you to join this fight with me – we can’t remain silent while Trump’s agenda drives up prices and hurts our communities.

– Scott Peters

name: Justice Democrats
subject: It’s official
body:
Justice Democrats is proud to have stood with Ilhan since she first ran for Congress in 2018, and we’re with her again in her 2026 re-election race.
#
It’s official, Peter: Progressive champion **Rep. Ilhan Omar is running for re-election in Minnesota’s 5th district!**
**[Will you split a grassroots contribution to kick her campaign off strong?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250410-io?refcode=em20250410-36575&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36575_1203867_Zgcc77&akid=36575%2E1203867%2EZgcc77)**
**Justice Democrats is proud to have stood with Ilhan since she first ran for Congress in 2018.** Together we made history when our people-powered movement elected Ilhan the first African refugee to become a member of Congress, the first woman of color to represent Minnesota, and one of the first two Muslim-American women elected to Congress.
Since winning her first election, Ilhan has been a fierce and consistent fighter on the issues we care about, from tackling the climate crisis, to ensuring healthcare for all, to achieving a true ceasefire in Gaza.
Now, she is helping to lead the charge to hold Donald Trump’s administration accountable and fight back against the billionaire oligarchs who seek to take from the working class for their own benefit.
But the truth is, winning re-election has never been easy. **Millions of dollars have been spent to unseat Ilhan over the years.** We have gone up against attacks from AIPAC, far-right special interests, conservative Democrats, and Donald Trump himself.
And each time we were victorious because we had a committed grassroots movement behind our campaign — a movement of folks doing what they can, when they can, to win over voters and fight for the progressive vision of the future that Ilhan and all of us share.
**[Let’s start Ilhan’s re-election campaign off on a high note and build the resources necessary to keep her fighting back against AIPAC and for us in Congress: split $5 or more between Justice Democrats and Ilhan’s campaign now →](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250410-io?refcode=em20250410-36575&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36575_1203867_Zgcc77&akid=36575%2E1203867%2EZgcc77)**
**[Contribute $5](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250410-io?refcode=em20250410-36575&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36575_1203867_Zgcc77&akid=36575%2E1203867%2EZgcc77)**
In solidarity,
Justice Democrats

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250410-36575-footer&t=2&refcode2=36575_1203867_Zgcc77&akid=36575%2E1203867%2EZgcc77)**
---

name: Mike Thompson
subject: Thank you for showing up.
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress [unsubscribe](https://support
body:
|
---
| | Peter,

I am so proud to have seen so many like-minded folks come together on Saturday and take to the streets to fight back against the recent unlawful and extreme actions of this Administration. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105611795/538816094/1162643037?source=EM_NBI_MT_20250410_EN_RallyTY_0_New_MikeThompson_NA_NA_RallyTY&refcode2=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDQ0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YmEzNzNlZS01OTE2LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jUHErwmDcjJ99iOcI5BglHx-U-qpR9Qli_78CitTU-Y=)
The people have made it clear — we do not stand for the actions of this Administration in trying to suppress our voices, undermine our democracy, and rule with extremism. Using our voices now more than ever, we must work together to preserve our democratic values.

Our work isn’t over, Peter. We must keep up this momentum and keep pushing back against this Administration. There’s too much at stake to slow down now.

Thank you again for showing up on Saturday. Your commitment to democracy is what we need right now to keep up the good work.

Let’s keep showing up together.

— Mike
---
|
---

| | Hey folks, Dennis Quaid here.

Here’s the thing: life doesn’t come with guarantees. The economy shifts, markets stumble, and years of hard work could slip through your fingers like sand.

Should it happen again—what’s your plan?

Think back just a few years. Inflation wasn’t the monster it feels like today. Prices made sense. The future felt steady.

But today? It’s a different story. Groceries cost more. Gas went up. And rising costs chip away at the value of your retirement savings every day.

But you don’t have to sit on the sidelines:
You’ve seen what happens when folks don’t prepare. It gets ugly. Savings accounts drain faster than expected, plans get downgraded, and peace of mind turns to heart-racing anxiety.

But it doesn’t have to be that way for you.

name: Marcy Kaptur
subject: Freshwater is life.
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP33/1/95655/images/kaptur-email-header-2.png]

Friend,

We must defend funding for the Great Lakes Restoration Initiative. This program is vital to our region and our nation, and I will do everything in my power to save it from Republican cuts.

Freshwater is life, friend, and the Great Lakes are the largest body of freshwater on Earth.

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kaptur25_em?amp;refcode=250410_mk_greatlakes_img [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kaptur25_em?amp;refcode=250410_mk_greatlakes_img]

They support a $7 billion fishery and a $16 billion boating industry and provide drinking water for 40 million people. They sustain ecosystems across the Midwest and the countless wildlife species that call these places home.

The Great Lakes Restoration Initiative is crucial to protecting this freshwater Kingdom, which holds 20% of the freshwater on Earth.

Thanks to GLRI, we've cleaned up toxic pollution in places like the Black River in Lorain, which is now one of the best walleye fishing areas in all of Lake Erie.

We're working to mitigate harmful algae blooms, like the one that shut off drinking water in Ohio and Michigan in 2014. And GLRI is leading the fight to prevent the spread of invasive carp, which would be disastrous for our Great Lakes economy and ecosystem.

Friend, this funding is critical for our state and our region. That's why it's supported by over 80% of Midwesterners and regularly passes with bipartisan support.

I'm proud to co-sponsor the GLRI Act of 2025, and I'll never stop fighting for our Great Lakes communities. Thank you for standing with me, friend.

— Marcy

Contribute [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kaptur25_em?amp;refcode=250410_mk_greatlakes]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP33/1/95655/images/kaptur-email-headshot.jpg]

About Marcy Kaptur
Marcy Kaptur is a fighter for high-wage jobs, growing a workforce to meet the needs of the 21st century, and protecting our region’s most vital natural resource, the Great Lakes. She’s worked with both parties to secure investments in our communities and has been a true friend of veterans, seniors, first responders and working people. She’s a voice for us all in the People’s House.
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kaptur25_em?refcode=emailfooter&refcode2=294c1a5a-5a16-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7] Give Monthly [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kaptur25_em?recurring=true&refcode=footermonthly&refcode2=294c1a5a-5a16-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7]
Follow Marcy

Shontel is fighting back.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105612144/538817455/1806897946?refcode=EM_F_250410heartless_N_TSB_90DO_5_NG_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZGViZTQ1NS01YjE2LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hPsqJLMXbn4O9YArE3k0CIPiIETq8lLRARJ0_BDy-fc=) Did you hear the news, Peter?

**Shontel Brown has introduced the Hunger Free Future Act, a bill to protect the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) from Republican plans to cut vital food assistance.**

This bill, which has already been backed by over 50 Democrats in Congress, fights back against the House Republican budget resolution that was passed in February, which called for $230 billion in cuts to SNAP. This is just another example of MAGA Republicans working to gut critical programs in order to give away massive tax breaks to billionaires.

**With this bill, Shontel is taking a stand against Republicans’ heartless efforts to gut food assistance so that their billionaire friends can buy another yacht.**

clean_body: Shontel is fighting back. = Did you hear the news, Peter? Shontel Brown has introduced the Hunger Free Future Act, a bill to protect the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) from Republican plans to cut vital food assistance. This bill, which has already been backed by over 50 Democrats in Congress, fights back against the House Republican budget resolution that was passed in February, which called for $230 billion in cuts to SNAP. This is just another example of MAGA Republicans working to gut critical programs in order to give away massive tax breaks to billionaires. With this bill, Shontel is taking a stand against Republicans’ heartless efforts to gut food assistance so that their billionaire friends can buy another yacht. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | | = | = | | = | = | | = Together, Team Shontel Brown Shontel Brown is proud to be elected as the Congresswoman for Ohio’s 11th District. She has spent the last decade serving the people of Ohio and building coalitions. As she enters her second full term in Congress, she will use that experience to continue the fight for affordable quality health care, jobs that pay fair wages, a strong public education system, and to preserve and protect our democracy for generations to come. name: Rebecca Cooke
subject: Van Orden showed us exactly who he is
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105612182/538817553/1706257898?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNTYyODM3YS01YjE2LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cgYMIdbuVVXcf0mL_as9qpLi3j2KzRw3KxCXuutbXSA=&emci=ec62837a-5b16-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=f562837a-5b16-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=5944223)
Well Peter, this morning, Derrick Van Orden showed us _exactly_ who he is. He voted to: ❌ Cut Medicaid, impacting 41,000 children in WI-03 ❌ Gut food assistance programs -- the same kind he relied on as a kid ❌ Give massive tax breaks to the ultra-wealthy In a district where multiple hospitals have shut down in just the past few months, where families are already struggling to afford care, and where more than 300,000 Wisconsinites live without health insurance, DVO voted to make life harder. It’s spineless. But as he loves to say: **We’ll remember in November.** We can -- and must -- do better. In Congress, I’ll fight for: ✅ Expanding Medicare to cover dental, vision, and hearing ✅ Holding Big Pharma accountable and lowering drug costs ✅ Incentivizing long-term care so seniors can age at home Derrick Van Orden has made it clear where he stands. Now it’s our turn to respond. Let’s send a message: **If you attack working families, you don’t belong in Congress.** [**If you're ready to flip this seat and fight for healthcare -- not tax cuts for billionaires -- chip in $3 or whatever you can today:**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105612183/538817554/-2087246159?refcode=em-4.10.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNTYyODM3YS01YjE2LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cgYMIdbuVVXcf0mL_as9qpLi3j2KzRw3KxCXuutbXSA=) | **[Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105612184/538817555/-2087246159?refcode=em-4.10.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNTYyODM3YS01YjE2LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cgYMIdbuVVXcf0mL_as9qpLi3j2KzRw3KxCXuutbXSA=)**
---
Grateful for your support,
Rebecca
|

name: Spanberger for Governor HQ
subject: re: our official opponent
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight for Progress PAC and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee
body:
Lt. Gov. Winsome Earle-Sears is the Republican nominee for Governor of Virginia and Abigail’s official opponent in the November 2025 race.
| Peter,

It’s official: Lt. Gov. Winsome Earle-Sears is the Republican nominee for Governor of Virginia and Abigail’s official opponent in the November 2025 race.

[_**We’ll explain more below, but first, will you contribute to Abigail’s campaign for Governor and Fight for Progress right now?**_](https://fight4progress.org/spanberger?t=IBCM6Qzts4lS&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-10_1&amount=25)
---
| [ CONTRIBUTE ](https://fight4progress.org/spanberger?t=u0k5vzm6J0bm&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-10_1&amount=25)
---
The stakes couldn’t be higher this November. Sears has spent her entire political career pushing extreme right-wing policies and backing Donald Trump at every turn. She’s repeatedly voiced her support for anti-choice measures to restrict women’s reproductive rights — including a near-total abortion ban — and just last week spoke at an anti-abortion rally in Richmond.

She’s not looking out for the people of Virginia. While Virginia's federal workers are facing instability and chaos from the Trump-Musk administration and DOGE’s cuts, Abigail’s opponent is standing behind Trump 100% and “doesn’t understand why” it’s a big deal that Virginians are being fired from their jobs. She literally told Virginians “don’t fret.”

Sears is just another rubber stamp for the MAGA agenda, and frankly, Virginians deserve better. Abigail will always put the people of Virginia first – she’ll work to lower costs, protect our fundamental rights, stand up for Virginia jobs, strengthen our public schools, and so much more.

But this race is set to be the most competitive in the country this November — Cook Political Report is calling it a total TOSS UP. Right now, Abigail Spanberger and Winsome Sears are neck and neck — and with Trump’s MAGA machine ready to flood Virginia with dark money, we can’t afford to fall behind.

[_**We need your help. Will you contribute today between Abigail’s campaign and Fight for Progress to help us flip the Governor’s office blue and elect Abigail Spanberger?**_](https://fight4progress.org/spanberger?t=VH1kHldfW5fq&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-10_1&amount=25)
| | | [ CONTRIBUTE $5 ](https://fight4progress.org/spanberger?t=sj9rpKNDMayw&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-10_1&amount=5)
---
| [ CONTRIBUTE $10 ](https://fight4progress.org/spanberger?t=bPb3b0JnXVWY&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-10_1&amount=10)
---
| [ CONTRIBUTE $25 ](https://fight4progress.org/spanberger?t=Rd64UHDnqwM2&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-10_1&amount=25)
---
| [ CONTRIBUTE $50 ](https://fight4progress.org/spanberger?t=1JImpwx5llTi&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-10_1&amount=50)
---
| [ OTHER AMOUNT ](https://fight4progress.org/spanberger?t=wtEueRc7w2PO&refcode=BLU_EM_FFP_2025-04-10_1)
---
Thank you,

Spanberger for Governor HQ

name: The Democrats
subject: How we'll put future donations to work:
body:
---
Derek, We're reaching out to ask if you can make a donation to the DNC today. But before we do, we want to tell you a little bit about what the donations from this grassroots team have already made possible and how we'll put future contributions to work. In this year alone, we:
* Invested heavily in the special elections in Wisconsin and Florida, which led to a huge win in Wisconsin and a dramatic overperformance in Florida.
* Sued Trump _twice_ , once for threatening free and fair elections and again for his illegal voter suppression executive order.
* Launched the People’s Cabinet, a project that brings together qualified policy experts, national and local leaders, and voices from our communities who will cut through the lies of the Trump administration.
* Announced a new War Room operation to take a more aggressive posture with rapid response.
* Held People’s Town Halls across the country, including in deep red districts — proving that Democrats will show up and listen to the American people, even if Republicans won’t.

And this is just the beginning. We are committed to continuing to organize and mobilize against Trump while electing Democrats at every level of government — leaders who will check Trump’s power in their cities, districts, and states. **That is the kind of work grassroots donations fund. And you might be surprised to hear this, but most of our grassroots donations come from emails just like this one. So now that we have explained where your donation has gone and where it is going, we're hoping you'll join thousands of Democrats making their first or next donation to the DNC. Use this link:** [Can Democrats running for office up and down the ballot count on you to contribute $3 to the Democratic National Committee today? We’ll put your contribution directly to work electing Democrats.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-april-2025?refcode=em_20250410_3161_fr&amount=3&refcode2=3161_22930333_VI0Oou&akid=3161%2E22930333%2EVI0Oou) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $3](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-april-2025?express_lane=true&amount=3&refcode=em_20250410_3161_fr&refcode2=3161_22930333_VI0Oou&akid=3161%2E22930333%2EVI0Oou)**

When we got married, we lived a normal American life. We had kids running around. Derrick was working on homes, helping out at the family ranch, even volunteering at rehab facilities across West Virginia – __a state ripped apart by opioids and alcoholism__. On top of running for local office, he also helped coached D1 football. Beyond the wins and losses, he wanted to teach young men how to __be good fathers, American__ ____patriots, and strong leaders__. But that all changed in a blink of an eye. **The life Derrick created for our family was** **FLIPPED UPSIDE DOWN OVERNIGHT.** **A SWAT team of Biden's FBI agents****busted down our front door. ** The kids were screaming. Derrick had his hands up. **I was CONFUSED, living in a NIGHTMARE.** Then I remembered – _Derrick went to Washington DC on January 6th to PEACEFULLY contest a stolen election_. _That’s what this was all about_. The Biden Regime labeled him an “insurrectionist” and that was it. **My husband was going to prison.** We tried to fight back. We argued entrapment. We argued violation of free speech. Nothing worked. The courts REFUSED to hear it. **Derrick’s fate was SEALED. ** He would be spending his nights in a cold, dark prison cell. I would be laying in bed alone wondering how I'm going to take care of four kids by myself. Months later, when Derrick was released on good behavior, _the man I saw come home was different_. He always had an interest in politics, _but going to prison this lit a fire inside of him I had never seen before_.
